title: Ex 1: The basics
text: assume already read Introduction installed wham dependencies. , able install using remotes pak packages: remotes::install_github("timjmiller/wham", dependencies=TRUE). pak::pkg_install("timjmiller/wham") Open R load wham package: clean, runnable .R script, look ex1_basics.R example_scripts folder wham package. can run entire example script : Let’s create directory analysis: WHAM built modifying ADMB-based ASAP model code (Legault Restrepo 1999), designed take ASAP3 .dat file input. generally assume wham existing ASAP3 .dat file. familiar ASAP3 input files, see ASAP documentation code. vignette, example ASAP3 input file provided. Read ASAP3 .dat file R:
code: library(wham) wham.dir <- find.package("wham") source(file.path(wham.dir, "example_scripts", "ex1_basics.R")) # choose a location to save output, otherwise will be saved in working directory write.dir <- "choose/where/to/save/output" #e.g., tempdir(check=TRUE) dir.create(write.dir) setwd(write.dir) path_to_examples <- system.file("extdata", package="wham") asap3 <- read_asap3_dat(file.path(path_to_examples,"ex1_SNEMAYT.dat"))

title: Ex 1: The basics
text: use prepare_wham_input() function specify model name settings differ ASAP3 file. first model use: recruitment model: random mean, S-R function (recruit_model = 2) recruitment deviations: independent random effects (NAA_re = list(sigma="rec", cor="iid")) selectivity: age-specific (fix sel=1 ages 4-5 fishery, age 4 index1, ages 2-4 index2) Note text printed console command. function attempts provide description assumptions made model configuration well changes default configurations recent versions. stock-recruit model options WHAM : 1 = random walk, 2 = random mean (default), 3 = Beverton-Holt, 4 = Ricker Note parameterization age-specific selectivity specific SNEMA yellowtail flounder. use age-specific selectivity parameters first three selectivity blocks. Selectivity blocks define selectivity configuration can used one fleets surveys set years model. prepare_wham_input() fixes age-specific parameters zero age classes without observations selectivity block applied. function configures age-specific parameters estimated. Generally may confounding selectivity parameters either fully-selected fishing mortality (fleets) catchability (surveys), selectivity parameters one ages need fixed allow convergence. initial fit selectivity parameters freely estimated can useful determining age(s) fix selectivity 1. code , already determined ages fix selectivity 1 (ages 4-5 fishery, age 4 index 1, ages 2-4 index 2). interested details options selectivity WHAM, see Example 4 prepare_wham_input().
code: input1 <- prepare_wham_input(asap3, recruit_model=2, model_name="Ex 1: SNEMA Yellowtail Flounder",   selectivity=list(model=rep("age-specific",3),      re=rep("none",3),      initial_pars=list(c(0.5,0.5,0.5,1,1,0.5),c(0.5,0.5,0.5,1,0.5,0.5),c(0.5,1,1,1,0.5,0.5)),      fix_pars=list(4:5,4,2:4)),   NAA_re = list(sigma="rec", cor="iid"))

what: 3. Fit model and check for convergence
title: Ex 1: The basics
text: default, fit_wham() uses 3 extra Newton steps reduce absolute value gradient (n.newton = 3) estimates standard errors derived parameters (.sdrep = TRUE). fit_wham() also retrospective analysis 7 peels default (.retro = TRUE, n.peels = 7). details, see fit_wham(). need check m1 converged (m1$opt$convergence 0, maximum absolute value gradient vector < 1e-06). Convergence issues may indicate model misspecified overparameterized. help diagnose problems, fit_wham() includes .check option run internal check_estimability function originally written Jim Thorson. .check = FALSE default. turn , set .check = TRUE. See fit_wham(). Now know model converged well, add retro peels
code: m1 <- fit_wham(input1, do.osa = F, do.retro = F) # turn off retro peels and OSA residuals to save time check_convergence(m1) #> stats:nlminb thinks the model has converged: mod$opt$convergence == 0 #>  #> Maximum gradient component:4.28e-13 #>  #> Max gradient parameter:F_pars #>  #> TMB:sdreport() was performed successfully for this model m1 <- do_retro_peels(m1)

previous_headings: 3. Fit model and check for convergence
title: Ex 1: The basics
text: second model, m2, like first, changes age composition likelihoods multinomial logistic normal (treating 0 observations missing): Check m2 converged: Now know model converged well, add retro peels third, m3, full state-space model numbers ages random effects (NAA_re$sigma = "rec+1"): Check m3 converged: Now know model converged well, add retro peels last, m4, like m3, changes age composition likelihoods logistic normal: Check m4 converged: max absolute gradient good. Try initially holding index age comp variance parameters fixed Convergence initial fit good Now initialize parameters best conditional estimates Now convergence good. Now, add One-step-ahead residuals model fit using make_osa_residuals function Store models together one (named) list: Since retrospective analyses take minutes run, may want save output later use:
code: input2 <- prepare_wham_input(asap3, recruit_model=2, model_name="Ex 1: SNEMA Yellowtail Flounder",   selectivity=list(model=rep("age-specific",3),      re=rep("none",3),      initial_pars=list(c(0.5,0.5,0.5,1,1,0.5),c(0.5,0.5,0.5,1,0.5,0.5),c(0.5,1,1,1,0.5,0.5)),      fix_pars=list(4:5,4,2:4)),   NAA_re = list(sigma="rec", cor="iid"),   age_comp = "logistic-normal-miss0") m2 <- fit_wham(input2, do.osa = F, do.retro = F) # turn off retro peels and OSA residuals to save time check_convergence(m2) #> stats:nlminb thinks the model has converged: mod$opt$convergence == 0 #>  #> Maximum gradient component:5.52e-12 #>  #> Max gradient parameter:index_paa_pars #>  #> TMB:sdreport() was performed successfully for this model m2 <- do_retro_peels(m2) input3 <- prepare_wham_input(asap3, recruit_model=2, model_name="Ex 1: SNEMA Yellowtail Flounder",   selectivity=list(model=rep("age-specific",3),      re=rep("none",3),      initial_pars=list(c(0.5,0.5,0.5,1,1,0.5),c(0.5,0.5,0.5,1,0.5,0.5),c(0.5,1,1,1,0.5,0.5)),      fix_pars=list(4:5,4,2:4)),   NAA_re = list(sigma="rec+1", cor="iid")) m3 <- fit_wham(input3, do.osa = F) # turn off OSA residuals to save time check_convergence(m3) #> stats:nlminb thinks the model has converged: mod$opt$convergence == 0 #>  #> Maximum gradient component:5.80e-12 #>  #> Max gradient parameter:logit_selpars #>  #> TMB:sdreport() was performed successfully for this model m3 <- do_retro_peels(m3) input4 <- prepare_wham_input(asap3, recruit_model=2, model_name="Ex 1: SNEMA Yellowtail Flounder",   selectivity=list(model=rep("age-specific",3),      re=rep("none",3),      initial_pars=list(c(0.5,0.5,0.5,1,1,0.5),c(0.5,0.5,0.5,1,0.5,0.5),c(0.5,1,1,1,0.5,0.5)),      fix_pars=list(4:5,4,2:4)),   NAA_re = list(sigma="rec+1", cor="iid"),   age_comp = "logistic-normal-miss0") m4 <- fit_wham(input4, do.osa = F) # turn off OSA residuals to save time check_convergence(m4) #> stats:nlminb thinks the model has NOT converged: mod$opt$convergence != 0 #>  #> Maximum gradient component:1.28e+09 #>  #> Max gradient parameter:index_paa_pars #>  #> TMB:sdreport() was performed for this model, but it appears hessian was not invertible input4_fixed <- input4 input4_fixed[["map"]][["index_paa_pars"]] <- factor(rep(NA, length(input4_fixed[["par"]][["index_paa_pars"]]))) m4_fixed <- fit_wham(input4_fixed, do.osa = F, do.retro = F) check_convergence(m4_fixed) #> stats:nlminb thinks the model has converged: mod$opt$convergence == 0 #>  #> Maximum gradient component:6.34e-10 #>  #> Max gradient parameter:F_pars #>  #> TMB:sdreport() was performed successfully for this model input4[["par"]] <- m4_fixed[["parList"]]  #best estimates so far m4_good <- fit_wham(input4, do.osa = F) # turn off OSA residuals to save time m4_good <- fit_wham(input4, do.osa = F) # turn off OSA residuals to save time check_convergence(m4_good) #> stats:nlminb thinks the model has converged: mod$opt$convergence == 0 #>  #> Maximum gradient component:6.34e-11 #>  #> Max gradient parameter:F_pars #>  #> TMB:sdreport() was performed successfully for this model m4_good <- make_osa_residuals(m4_good) #returns the model with the residuals added. mods <- list(m1=m1, m2=m2, m3=m3, m4=m4_good) save("mods", file="ex1_models.RData")

title: Ex 1: The basics

However, example models alternative likelihoods age composition observations comparable due differences observations defined. Still, models treat age composition observations way can compared evaluate whether stochasticity abundances age provides better performance, .e. m1 vs. m3 (multinomial) m2 vs. m4 (logistic normal). comparison plots stored default compare_png folder.

title: Ex 2: Recruitment linked to an environmental covariate (Cold Pool Index)
text: Open R load wham package: clean, runnable .R script, look ex2_CPI_recruitment.R example_scripts folder wham package. can run entire example script : Let’s create directory analysis: WHAM originally built modifying ADMB-based ASAP model code (Legault Restrepo 1999), designed take ASAP3 .dat file input. generally assume wham existing ASAP3 .dat file. familiar ASAP3 input files, see ASAP documentation code. vignette, example ASAP3 input file provided, ex2_SNEMAYT.dat. also need data file environmental covariate, Cold Pool Index, CPI.csv. Read ex2_SNEMAYT.dat CPI.csv R: generally abbreviate ‘environmental covariate’ ecov code. example, ecov data file columns observations (CPI), standard error (CPI_sigma), year (Year). Observations year always required. Standard error can treated fixed/data yearly values () one overall value shared among years. can also estimated parameter(s), likewise either yearly values one overall value.
code: library(wham) wham.dir <- find.package("wham") source(file.path(wham.dir, "example_scripts", "ex2_CPI_recruitment.R")) # choose a location to save output, otherwise will be saved in working directory write.dir <- "choose/where/to/save/output" #e.g., tempdir(check=TRUE) dir.create(write.dir) setwd(write.dir) wham.dir <- find.package("wham") asap3 <- read_asap3_dat(file.path(wham.dir,"extdata","ex2_SNEMAYT.dat")) env.dat <- read.csv(file.path(wham.dir,"extdata","CPI.csv"), header=T) head(env.dat) #>   Year     CPI CPI_sigma #> 1 1973  0.5988    0.2838 #> 2 1974 -0.1760    0.2465 #> 3 1975 -1.1887    0.2539 #> 4 1976 -0.7938    0.2634 #> 5 1977 -0.6771    0.1576 #> 6 1978 -1.5195    0.2045

title: Ex 2: Recruitment linked to an environmental covariate (Cold Pool Index)
text: Now specify 7 models treat recruitment, CPI process, CPI affects recruitment: Look model table. Ecov_how recent character string approach defining environmental effects recruitment. specify options modeling recruitment environmental covariate(s) using prepare_wham_input() function. WHAM provides 4 options recruitment (recruit_model): random walk, random mean, Beverton-Holt, Ricker. environmental covariate options fed prepare_wham_input() list, ecov: currently 2 options ecov process model (ecov$process_model): 1) random walk ('rw'), 2) autoregressive ('ar1'). Recent versions WHAM now specify effects covariates recruitment, natural mortality, catchability, movement using character strings. recruitment specify recruitment_how mechanistic affect recruitment, time lag covariate recruitment, order orthogonal polynomial effect. options mechanistic effect follow Iles Beverton (1998) Xu et al. (2018): “controlling” (density-independent mortality), “limiting” (carrying capacity, e.g. ecov determines amount suitable habitat), “lethal” (threshold, .e. R –> 0 ecov value), “masking” (metabolic/growth, ecov decreases dR/dS), “directive” (e.g., behavioral). Ecov_how specify lag 1 CPI CPI year t affects recruitment year t + 1. also specify first order polynomial (“linear” “poly-1”). effect covariate, still keeping state-space model estimation covariate, Ecov_how= “none”. can set ecov = NULL process_model= NA fit model without environmental covariate data, note fit ecov data even models without ecov effect recruitment (m1 m3) can compare via AIC (need data likelihood). Options described set_ecov help page. mechanistic effects options implemented every recruitment model.
code: Ecov_how <- paste0(   c("none", "controlling-", "none", "limiting-", "limiting-", "controlling-", "controlling-"),    c("", "lag-1-", "", rep("lag-1-",4)),   c("", "linear", "", rep("linear", 4)))  df.mods <- data.frame(Recruitment = c(2,2,3,3,3,3,4),                       Ecov_process = c(rep("rw",4),rep("ar1",3)),                       Ecov_how = Ecov_how, stringsAsFactors=FALSE) n.mods <- dim(df.mods)[1] df.mods$Model <- paste0("m",1:n.mods) df.mods <- dplyr::select(df.mods, Model, tidyselect::everything()) # moves Model to first col df.mods #>   Model Recruitment Ecov_process                 Ecov_how #> 1    m1           2           rw                     none #> 2    m2           2           rw controlling-lag-1-linear #> 3    m3           3           rw                     none #> 4    m4           3           rw    limiting-lag-1-linear #> 5    m5           3          ar1    limiting-lag-1-linear #> 6    m6           3          ar1 controlling-lag-1-linear #> 7    m7           4          ar1 controlling-lag-1-linear m=1 # example for first model   ecov <- list(     label = "CPI",     mean = as.matrix(env.dat$CPI),     logsigma = as.matrix(log(env.dat$CPI_sigma)),     year = env.dat$Year,     use_obs = matrix(1, ncol=1, nrow=dim(env.dat)[1]), # use all obs (=1)     process_model = df.mods$Ecov_process[m], # "rw" or "ar1"     recruitment_how = matrix(df.mods$Ecov_how[m],1,1)) #matrix for number of stocks (1) and number of Ecovs (1) ?set_ecov

title: Ex 2: Recruitment linked to an environmental covariate (Cold Pool Index)
code: for(m in 1:n.mods){   # set up environmental covariate data and model options   ecov <- list(     label = "CPI",     mean = as.matrix(env.dat$CPI),     logsigma = as.matrix(log(env.dat$CPI_sigma)),     year = env.dat$Year,     use_obs = matrix(1, ncol=1, nrow=dim(env.dat)[1]), # use all obs (=1)     process_model = df.mods$Ecov_process[m], # "rw" or "ar1"     recruitment_how = matrix(df.mods$Ecov_how[m],1,1)) #matrix for number of stocks (1) and number of Ecovs (1)    # (not used in this vignette) can set ecov = NULL to fit model without ecov data   if(is.na(df.mods$ecov_process[m])) ecov = NULL     # generate wham input from ASAP3 and ecov data   input <- prepare_wham_input(asap3, recruit_model = df.mods$Recruitment[m],                               model_name = "Ex 2: SNEMA Yellowtail Flounder with CPI effects on R",                               ecov = ecov,                               NAA_re = list(sigma="rec+1", cor="iid"),                               age_comp = "logistic-normal-pool0") # logistic normal pool 0 obs      # Selectivity = logistic, not age-specific as in ex1   #   2 pars per block instead of n.ages   #   sel pars of indices 4/5 fixed at 1.5, 0.1 (specified via neg phase in ex2_SNEMAYT.dat)   input$par$logit_selpars[1:4,7:8] <- 0 # last 2 rows will not be estimated (mapped to NA)    # Fit model   mod <- fit_wham(input, do.retro=TRUE, do.osa=TRUE)    # Save model   saveRDS(mod, file=paste0(df.mods$Model[m],".rds"))    # Plot output in new subfolder   plot_wham_output(mod=mod, dir.main=file.path(getwd(),df.mods$Model[m]), out.type='html') }

title: Ex 2: Recruitment linked to an environmental covariate (Cold Pool Index)
text: Collect models list. need check models converged. maximum absolute gradient close 0 SE estimates calculable (invertible Hessian, TMB::sdreport() succeeds). models seem converged positive definite Hessian.
code: mod.list <- paste0(df.mods$Model,".rds") mods <- lapply(mod.list, readRDS) vign2_conv <- lapply(mods, function(x) capture.output(check_convergence(x))) for(m in 1:n.mods) cat(paste0("Model ",m,":"), vign2_conv[[m]], "", sep='\n') #> Model 1: #> stats:nlminb thinks the model has converged: mod$opt$convergence == 0 #> Maximum gradient component: 1.74e-10  #> Max gradient parameter: logit_selpars  #> TMB:sdreport() was performed successfully for this model #>  #> Model 2: #> stats:nlminb thinks the model has converged: mod$opt$convergence == 0 #> Maximum gradient component: 1.88e-10  #> Max gradient parameter: logit_selpars  #> TMB:sdreport() was performed successfully for this model #>  #> Model 3: #> stats:nlminb thinks the model has converged: mod$opt$convergence == 0 #> Maximum gradient component: 4.17e-10  #> Max gradient parameter: logit_selpars  #> TMB:sdreport() was performed successfully for this model #>  #> Model 4: #> stats:nlminb thinks the model has converged: mod$opt$convergence == 0 #> Maximum gradient component: 1.35e-10  #> Max gradient parameter: logit_selpars  #> TMB:sdreport() was performed successfully for this model #>  #> Model 5: #> stats:nlminb thinks the model has converged: mod$opt$convergence == 0 #> Maximum gradient component: 1.45e-10  #> Max gradient parameter: logit_selpars  #> TMB:sdreport() was performed successfully for this model #>  #> Model 6: #> stats:nlminb thinks the model has converged: mod$opt$convergence == 0 #> Maximum gradient component: 1.34e-10  #> Max gradient parameter: logit_selpars  #> TMB:sdreport() was performed successfully for this model #>  #> Model 7: #> stats:nlminb thinks the model has converged: mod$opt$convergence == 0 #> Maximum gradient component: 1.72e-10  #> Max gradient parameter: logit_selpars  #> TMB:sdreport() was performed successfully for this model

title: Ex 2: Recruitment linked to an environmental covariate (Cold Pool Index)
text: Let’s first make results table prettier. Now get convergence information. calculate AIC Mohn’s rho converged models. Print save results table. m6 lowest AIC (Bev-Holt recruitment, CPI modeled AR1, controlling effect CPI recruitment), m5 m7 similar AIC values.
code: df.mods$Recruitment <- dplyr::recode(df.mods$Recruitment, `2`='Random', `3`='Bev-Holt', `4`='Ricker') df.mods$Ecov_how <- c("---", "Controlling", "---", "Limiting", "Limiting", "Controlling", "Controlling") opt_conv = 1-sapply(mods, function(x) x$opt$convergence) ok_sdrep = sapply(mods, function(x) if(x$na_sdrep==FALSE & !is.na(x$na_sdrep)) 1 else 0) df.mods$conv <- as.logical(opt_conv) df.mods$pdHess <- as.logical(ok_sdrep) df.mods$NLL <- sapply(mods, function(x) round(x$opt$objective,3)) not_conv <- !df.mods$conv | !df.mods$pdHess mods2 <- mods mods2[not_conv] <- NULL df.aic.tmp <- as.data.frame(compare_wham_models(mods2, table.opts=list(sort=FALSE, calc.rho=T))$tab) df.aic <- df.aic.tmp[FALSE,] ct = 1 for(i in 1:n.mods){   if(not_conv[i]){     df.aic[i,] <- rep(NA,5)   } else {     df.aic[i,] <- df.aic.tmp[ct,]     ct <- ct + 1   } } df.mods <- cbind(df.mods, df.aic) df.mods <- df.mods[order(df.mods$dAIC, na.last=TRUE),] df.mods[is.na(df.mods$AIC), c('dAIC','AIC','rho_R','rho_SSB','rho_Fbar')] <- "---" rownames(df.mods) <- NULL df.mods save(df.mods, file="vign2_res.RData") #>   Recruitment Ecov_process    Ecov_how Model conv pdHess      NLL  Rsig dAIC #> 1    Bev-Holt          ar1 Controlling    m6 TRUE   TRUE -824.765 0.688  0.0 #> 2    Bev-Holt          ar1    Limiting    m5 TRUE   TRUE -824.272 0.694  1.0 #> 3      Ricker          ar1 Controlling    m7 TRUE   TRUE -824.250 0.699  1.0 #> 4    Bev-Holt           rw    Limiting    m4 TRUE   TRUE -813.544 0.693 20.4 #> 5      Random           rw Controlling    m2 TRUE   TRUE -810.097 0.780 25.3 #> 6    Bev-Holt           rw         ---    m3 TRUE   TRUE -808.177 0.859 29.1 #> 7      Random           rw         ---    m1 TRUE   TRUE -803.139 0.997 37.2 #>       AIC  rho_R rho_SSB rho_Fbar #> 1 -1509.5 0.2072  0.1070  -0.1334 #> 2 -1508.5 0.2152  0.1126  -0.1382 #> 3 -1508.5 0.2044  0.1030  -0.1302 #> 4 -1489.1 0.2149  0.1129  -0.1384 #> 5 -1484.2 0.2312  0.1196  -0.1459 #> 6 -1480.4 0.2319  0.1094  -0.1351 #> 7 -1472.3 0.2592  0.1169  -0.1452

title: Ex 2: Recruitment linked to an environmental covariate (Cold Pool Index)
text: Models included effect Cold Pool Index recruitment strongly supported AIC models without CPI effects (m2 m4-7 lower AIC m1 m3). Note can compare models without CPI effect recruitment using AIC also fit CPI data models without effect (m1 m3). Comparing m4 m5 demonstrates CPI best modeled AR1 process (m5) instead random walk (m4), since difference two models m5 lower AIC. one-step-ahead residuals CPI m5 (right) similar distribution. linear trend OSA residuals observed value m5 due best prediction next observation near mean process case estimated autocorrelation parameter near 0:  saw table results, models produced similar Mohn’s ρ\rho values SSB, F, recruitment. relative retrospective peels model without SSB CPI effects recruitment (m1, left) best model included CPI SSB effects recruitment, (m6, right).

title: Ex 2: Recruitment linked to an environmental covariate (Cold Pool Index)
text: Beverton-Holt stock-recruitment assumption preferred random recruitment (m3 lower AIC m1). Models included Bev-Holt CPI effects recruitment lower AIC model Bev-Holt without CPI (m4 vs. m3). Adding CPI effect Bev-Holt explains variability around stock-recruit curve, resulted m4 (right) estimating lower σR\sigma_R m3 (left).

title: Ex 3: Projecting / forecasting random effects
text: Open R load wham package: clean, runnable .R script, look ex3_projections.R example_scripts folder wham package. can run entire example script : Let’s create directory analysis: need data files example 2. Read ex2_SNEMAYT.dat CPI.csv:
code: library(wham) wham.dir <- find.package("wham") source(file.path(wham.dir, "example_scripts", "ex3_projections.R")) # choose a location to save output, otherwise will be saved in working directory write.dir <- "choose/where/to/save/output" # need to change e.g., tempdir(check=TRUE) dir.create(write.dir) setwd(write.dir) wham.dir <- find.package("wham") asap3 <- read_asap3_dat(file.path(wham.dir,"extdata","ex2_SNEMAYT.dat")) env.dat <- read.csv(file.path(wham.dir,"extdata","CPI.csv"), header=T)

title: Ex 3: Projecting / forecasting random effects
text: Setup model m5 example 2: full state-space model (numbers--age random effects ages, NAA_re = list(sigma='rec+1',cor='iid')) logistic normal age compositions (age_comp = "logistic-normal-pool0") Beverton-Holt recruitment (recruit_model = 3) Cold Pool Index (CPI) fit AR1 process (ecov$process_model = "ar1") CPI “controlling” (density-independent mortality, Iles Beverton (1998)) effect recruitment (ecov$= "recruit", ecov$= 1)
code: env <- list(   label = "CPI",   mean = as.matrix(env.dat$CPI), # CPI observations   logsigma = as.matrix(log(env.dat$CPI_sigma)), # CPI standard error is given/fixed as data   year = env.dat$Year,   use_obs = matrix(1, ncol=1, nrow=dim(env.dat)[1]), # use all obs (=1)   process_model = "ar1", # fit CPI as AR1 process   recruitment_how = matrix("limiting-lag-1-linear")) # limiting (carrying capacity), CPI in year t affects recruitment in year t+1  input <- prepare_wham_input(asap3, recruit_model = 3,                             model_name = "Ex 3: Projections",                             ecov = env,                             NAA_re = list(sigma="rec+1", cor="iid"),                             age_comp = "logistic-normal-pool0") # logistic normal pool 0 obs  # selectivity = logistic, not age-specific #   2 pars per block instead of n.ages #   sel pars of indices 4/5 fixed at 1.5, 0.1 (neg phase in .dat file) input$par$logit_selpars[1:4,7:8] <- 0 # original code started selpars at 0 (last 2 rows are fixed)

title: Ex 3: Projecting / forecasting random effects
text: two options projecting WHAM model: Fit model without projections add projections afterward Add projections initial model fit (.proj = TRUE) two code blocks equivalent; .proj = TRUE, fit_wham() fits model without projections calls project_wham() add . example choose option #1 going add several different projections model, mod. save projected model list, mod_proj.
code: # don't run mod <- fit_wham(input) # default do.proj=FALSE mod_proj <- project_wham(mod) # don't run mod_proj <- fit_wham(input, do.proj = TRUE) # run mod <- fit_wham(input) saveRDS(mod, file="m5.rds") # save unprojected model

title: Ex 3: Projecting / forecasting random effects
text: Projection options specifed using proj.opts input project_wham(). default settings project 3 years (n.yrs = 3), use average maturity-, weight-, natural mortality--age last 5 model years calculate reference points (avg.yrs), use fishing mortality last model year (use.last.F = TRUE), continue ecov process model (cont.ecov = TRUE). options also described project_wham() help page. WHAM implements four options handling environmental covariate(s) projections. Exactly one must specified proj.opts ecov model: (Default) Continue ecov process model (e.g. random walk, AR1). Set cont.ecov = TRUE. WHAM estimate ecov process projection years (.e. continue random walk / AR1 process). Use last year ecov. Set use.last.ecov = TRUE. WHAM use ecov value terminal year population model projections. Use average ecov. Provide avg.yrs.ecov, vector specifying years average environmental covariate(s) projections. Specify ecov. Provide proj.ecov, matrix user-specified environmental covariate(s) use projections. Dimensions must number projection years (proj.opts$n.yrs) x number ecovs (ncols(ecov$mean)). Note options, original model fit ecov years beyond population model, WHAM use already-fit ecov values projections. ecov model extended least proj.opts$n.yrs years beyond population model, none need specified. WHAM implements six options handling fishing mortality projections. Exactly one must specified proj.opts: (Default) Use last year F. Set use.last.F = TRUE. WHAM use F terminal model year projections. Use average F. Set use.avg.F = TRUE. WHAM use F averaged proj.opts$avg.yrs projections (done M-, maturity-, weight--age). Use F X% SPR. Set use.FXSPR = TRUE. WHAM calculate apply F X% SPR, X set input$data$percentSPR (default = 40%). also percentFXSPR Specify F. Provide proj.F, F vector length = proj.opts$n.yrs. Specify catch. Provide proj.catch, vector aggregate catch length = proj.opts$n.yrs. WHAM calculate F across fleets apply specified catch. Use FMSY. Set use.FMSY = TRUE. WHAM calculate apply F MSY. check make sure stock-recruit model assumed , , F X% SPR used instead. WHAM can also use average “apparent survival” deviations /recruitment deviations apparent survival recruitment deviations projections allows user specify years average different inputs. proj.opts$proj_NAA_opt can specified tell treat apparent survival deviations. proj.opts$proj_R_opt can specify treat recruitment deviations. avg.yrs.R avg.yrs.NAA specify years average deviations using option. Save projected models
code: # save projected models in a list mod_proj <- list()  # default settings spelled out mod_proj[[1]] <- project_wham(mod, proj.opts=list(n.yrs=3, use.last.F=TRUE, use.avg.F=FALSE,    use.FXSPR=FALSE, use.FMSY=FALSE, proj.F=NULL, proj.catch=NULL, avg.yrs=NULL,    cont.ecov=TRUE, use.last.ecov=FALSE, avg.ecov.yrs=NULL, proj.ecov=NULL, cont.Mre=NULL,    avg.rec.yrs=NULL, percentFXSPR=100, percentFMSY=100))  # equivalent # mod_proj[[1]] <- project_wham(mod) # 5 years, use average ecov from 1992-1996 mod_proj[[2]] <- project_wham(mod, proj.opts=list(n.yrs=5, use.last.F=TRUE, use.avg.F=FALSE,               use.FXSPR=FALSE, proj.F=NULL, proj.catch=NULL, avg.yrs=NULL,               cont.ecov=FALSE, use.last.ecov=FALSE, avg.ecov.yrs=1992:1996, proj.ecov=NULL)) # equivalent # mod_proj[[2]] <- project_wham(mod, proj.opts=list(n.yrs=5, avg.ecov.yrs=1992:1996))  # 5 years, use ecov from last year (2011) mod_proj[[3]] <- project_wham(mod, proj.opts=list(n.yrs=5, use.last.F=TRUE, use.avg.F=FALSE,               use.FXSPR=FALSE, proj.F=NULL, proj.catch=NULL, avg.yrs=NULL,               cont.ecov=FALSE, use.last.ecov=TRUE, avg.ecov.yrs=NULL, proj.ecov=NULL)) # equivalent # mod_proj[[3]] <- project_wham(mod, proj.opts=list(n.yrs=5, use.last.ecov=TRUE))  # 5 years, specify high CPI ~ 0.5 # note: again, need 5 years of CPI because in general, the lag of the CPI effect may not be known (no effect) or it may differ by effect on various population attributes, mod_proj[[4]] <- project_wham(mod, proj.opts=list(n.yrs=5, use.last.F=TRUE, use.avg.F=FALSE,               use.FXSPR=FALSE, proj.F=NULL, proj.catch=NULL, avg.yrs=NULL,               cont.ecov=FALSE, use.last.ecov=FALSE, avg.ecov.yrs=NULL,                proj.ecov=matrix(c(0.5,0.7,0.4,0.5,0.55),ncol=1))) # equivalent # mod_proj[[4]] <- project_wham(mod, proj.opts=list(n.yrs=5, proj.ecov=matrix(c(0.5,0.7,0.4,0.5,0.55),ncol=1)))  # 5 years, specify low CPI ~ -1.5 # note: again, need 5 years of CPI because in general, the lag of the CPI effect may not be known (no effect) or it may differ by effect on various population attributes, mod_proj[[5]] <- project_wham(mod, proj.opts=list(n.yrs=5, use.last.F=TRUE, use.avg.F=FALSE,               use.FXSPR=FALSE, proj.F=NULL, proj.catch=NULL, avg.yrs=NULL,               cont.ecov=FALSE, use.last.ecov=FALSE, avg.ecov.yrs=NULL,                proj.ecov=matrix(c(-1.6,-1.3,-1,-1.2,-1.25),ncol=1)))  # equivalent # mod_proj[[5]] <- project_wham(mod, proj.opts=list(n.yrs=5, proj.ecov=matrix(c(-1.6,-1.3,-1,-1.2,-1.25),ncol=1))) # 5 years, specify catch mod_proj[[6]] <- project_wham(mod, proj.opts=list(n.yrs=5, use.last.F=FALSE, use.avg.F=FALSE,               use.FXSPR=FALSE, proj.F=NULL, proj.catch=c(10, 2000, 1000, 3000, 20), avg.yrs=NULL,               cont.ecov=TRUE, use.last.ecov=FALSE, avg.ecov.yrs=NULL, proj.ecov=NULL)) # equivalent # mod_proj[[6]] <- project_wham(mod, proj.opts=list(n.yrs=5, proj.catch=c(10, 2000, 1000, 3000, 20)))  # 5 years, specify F mod_proj[[7]] <- project_wham(mod, proj.opts=list(n.yrs=5, use.last.F=FALSE, use.avg.F=FALSE,               use.FXSPR=FALSE, proj.F=c(0.001, 1, 0.5, .1, .2), proj.catch=NULL, avg.yrs=NULL,               cont.ecov=TRUE, use.last.ecov=FALSE, avg.ecov.yrs=NULL, proj.ecov=NULL)) # equivalent # mod_proj[[7]] <- project_wham(mod, proj.opts=list(n.yrs=5, proj.F=c(0.001, 1, 0.5, .1, .2)))  # 5 years, use FXSPR mod_proj[[8]] <- project_wham(mod, proj.opts=list(n.yrs=5, use.last.F=FALSE, use.avg.F=FALSE,               use.FXSPR=TRUE, proj.F=NULL, proj.catch=NULL, avg.yrs=NULL,               cont.ecov=TRUE, use.last.ecov=FALSE, avg.ecov.yrs=NULL, proj.ecov=NULL)) # equivalent # mod_proj[[8]] <- project_wham(mod, proj.opts=list(n.yrs=5, use.FXSPR=TRUE))  # 3 years, use avg F (avg.yrs defaults to last 5 years, 2007-2011) mod_proj[[9]] <- project_wham(mod, proj.opts=list(n.yrs=3, use.last.F=FALSE, use.avg.F=TRUE,               use.FXSPR=FALSE, proj.F=NULL, proj.catch=NULL, avg.yrs=NULL,               cont.ecov=TRUE, use.last.ecov=FALSE, avg.ecov.yrs=NULL, proj.ecov=NULL)) # equivalent # mod_proj[[9]] <- project_wham(mod, proj.opts=list(use.avg.F=TRUE))  # 10 years, use avg F 1992-1996 mod_proj[[10]] <- project_wham(mod, proj.opts=list(n.yrs=10, use.last.F=FALSE, use.avg.F=TRUE,               use.FXSPR=FALSE, proj.F=NULL, proj.catch=NULL, avg.yrs=1992:1996,               cont.ecov=TRUE, use.last.ecov=FALSE, avg.ecov.yrs=NULL, proj.ecov=NULL)) # equivalent # mod_proj[[10]] <- project_wham(mod, proj.opts=list(n.yrs=10, use.avg.F=TRUE, avg.yrs=1992:1996))  # 5 years, use $F_{MSY}$ mod_proj[[11]] <- project_wham(mod, proj.opts=list(n.yrs=5, use.last.F=FALSE, use.avg.F=FALSE,               use.FMSY=TRUE, proj.F=NULL, proj.catch=NULL, avg.yrs=NULL,               cont.ecov=TRUE, use.last.ecov=FALSE, avg.ecov.yrs=NULL, proj.ecov=NULL)) # default settings: 3 years, use last F, continue ecov, but projected recruitment deviations=0 and average NAA deviations (1992-1996) projected mod_proj[[12]] <- project_wham(mod, proj.opts=list(n.yrs=3, use.last.F=TRUE, use.avg.F=FALSE,               use.FXSPR=FALSE, proj.F=NULL, proj.catch=NULL, avg.yrs=NULL,               cont.ecov=TRUE, use.last.ecov=FALSE, avg.ecov.yrs=NULL, proj.ecov=NULL, proj_R_opt = 4, proj_NAA_opt = 2, avg.yrs.NAA = list(list(1992:1996))))  # default settings: 3 years, use last F, continue ecov, but projected recruitment deviations are averged over 1992-1996 and no NAA deviations projected mod_proj[[13]] <- project_wham(mod, proj.opts=list(n.yrs=3, use.last.F=TRUE, use.avg.F=FALSE,               use.FXSPR=FALSE, proj.F=NULL, proj.catch=NULL, avg.yrs=NULL,               cont.ecov=TRUE, use.last.ecov=FALSE, avg.ecov.yrs=NULL, proj.ecov=NULL, proj_R_opt = 3, proj_NAA_opt = 3, avg.yrs.R = list(1992:1996)))  # default settings: 3 years, use last F, continue ecov, but projected recruitment converges to that used for 40%SPR BRPS and no NAA deviations projected mod_proj[[14]] <- project_wham(mod, proj.opts=list(n.yrs=3, use.last.F=TRUE, use.avg.F=FALSE,               use.FXSPR=FALSE, proj.F=NULL, proj.catch=NULL, avg.yrs=NULL,               cont.ecov=TRUE, use.last.ecov=FALSE, avg.ecov.yrs=NULL, proj.ecov=NULL, proj_R_opt = 2, proj_NAA_opt = 3, avg.yrs.R = list(1992:1996))) saveRDS(mod_proj, file="m5_proj.rds")

title: Ex 4: Selectivity with time- and age-varying random effects
text: Open R load wham package: clean, runnable .R script, look ex4_selectivity.R example_scripts folder wham package. can run entire example script : Let’s create directory analysis: need data files example 1. Read ex1_SNEMAYT.dat:
code: library(wham) wham.dir <- find.package("wham") source(file.path(wham.dir, "example_scripts", "ex4_selectivity.R")) # choose a location to save output, otherwise will be saved in working directory write.dir <- "choose/where/to/save/output" # need to change e.g., tempdir(check=TRUE) dir.create(write.dir) setwd(write.dir) asap3 <- read_asap3_dat(file.path(wham.dir,"extdata","ex1_SNEMAYT.dat"))

title: Ex 4: Selectivity with time- and age-varying random effects
text: going run 8 models differ selectivity options:
code: # m1-m5 logistic, m6-m9 age-specific sel_model <- c(rep("logistic",3), rep("age-specific",5))  # time-varying options for each of 3 blocks (b1 = fleet, b2-3 = indices) sel_re <- list(c("none","none","none"), # m1-m4 logistic                 c("iid","none","none"), #               c("ar1","none","none"), #can't get a converged model for logistic with two re and two fe for                 c("2dar1","none","none"),                 c("none","none","none"), # m4-m8 age-specific                 c("iid","none","none"),                 c("ar1","none","none"), #will map all age-specific (mean) parameters to one estimated value                 c("ar1_y","none","none"),                 c("2dar1","none","none")) n.mods <- length(sel_re)  # summary data frame df.mods <- data.frame(Model=paste0("m",1:n.mods),      Selectivity=sel_model, # Selectivity model (same for all blocks)     Block_1_re=sapply(sel_re, function(x) x[[1]])) # Block 1 random effects rownames(df.mods) <- NULL  df.mods #>   Model  Selectivity Block_1_re #> 1    m1     logistic       none #> 2    m2     logistic        iid #> 3    m3     logistic      2dar1 #> 4    m4 age-specific       none #> 5    m5 age-specific        iid #> 6    m6 age-specific        ar1 #> 7    m7 age-specific      ar1_y #> 8    m8 age-specific      2dar1

title: Ex 4: Selectivity with time- and age-varying random effects
text: ASAP data file specifies selectivity options (model, initial parameter values, parameters fix/estimate). WHAM uses default order facilitate running ASAP models. see currently specified selectivity options asap3: specify WHAM model prepare_wham_input(), can overwrite selectivity options ASAP data file optional list argument selectivity. selectivity model chosen via selectivity$model: Regardless selectivity model used, incorporate time-varying selectivity estimating mean selectivity parameter, μas\mu^{s}_a, (random effect) deviations mean, δa,y\delta_{,y}. estimate selectivity parameters, sa,ys_{,y}, logit-scale (possibly) lower upper limits: sa,y=lower+upper−lower1+e−(μas+δa,y)s_{,y} = \mathrm{lower} + \frac{\mathrm{upper} - \mathrm{lower}}{1 + e^{-(\mu^{s}_a + \delta_{,y})}} deviations, 𝛅\boldsymbol{\delta}, follow 2-dimensional AR(1) process defined parameters σs2\sigma^2_s, ρa\rho_a, ρy\rho_y: 𝛅∼MVN(0,Σ)\boldsymbol{\delta} \sim \mathrm{MVN}(0,\Sigma)Σ=σs2(Ra⊗Ry)\Sigma = \sigma^2_s(\mathrm{R}_a \otimes \mathrm{R}_y)Ra,*=ρa|−*|R_{,^*} = \rho_a^{\vert - ^* \vert}Ry,y*=ρy|y−y*|R_{y,y^*} = \rho_y^{\vert y - y^* \vert} Mean selectivity parameters can initialized different values ASAP file selectivity$initial_pars. Parameters can fixed initial values specifying selectivity$fix_pars. Finally, specify time-varying (random effects) selectivity parameters (selectivity$re): loop fit models, first lets define initial parameter values fixed. needed specifically age-specific selectivity models. Now can run models loop:
code: asap3$dat$sel_block_assign # 1 fleet, all years assigned to block 1 #> NULL # by default each index gets its own selectivity block (here, blocks 2 and 3)  asap3$dat$sel_block_option # fleet selectivity (1 block), 2 = logistic #> NULL asap3$dat$index_sel_option # index selectivity (2 blocks), 2 = logistic #> NULL  asap3$dat$sel_ini # fleet sel initial values (col1), estimation phase (-1 = fix) #> NULL asap3$dat$index_sel_ini # index sel initial values (col1), estimation phase (-1 = fix) #> NULL #initial pars for logistic or age-specfic selectivity initial_pars <- c(rep(list(list(c(2,0.3),c(2,0.3),c(2,0.3))),3), rep(list(list(c(0.1,0.5,0.5,1,1,1),c(0.5,0.5,0.5,1,1,0.5),c(0.5,0.5,1,1,1,1))),5)) #which pars to fix for age-specific selectivity fix_pars <- c(list(NULL,NULL,NULL), rep(list(list(4:6,4:5,3:6)),5)) #make a list of fits to fill in mods <- list()  for(m in 1:n.mods){     # overwrite initial parameter values in ASAP data file (ex1_SNEMAYT.dat)     input <- prepare_wham_input(asap3, model_name=paste(paste0("Model ",m), sel_model[m], paste(sel_re[[m]], collapse="-"), sep=": "), recruit_model=2,                 selectivity=list(model=rep(sel_model[m],3), re=sel_re[[m]], initial_pars=initial_pars[[m]], fix_pars = fix_pars[[m]]),                 NAA_re = list(sigma='rec+1',cor='iid'),                 age_comp = "logistic-normal-miss0") # logistic normal, treat 0 obs as missing      # fit model     mods[[m]] <- fit_wham(input, do.check=T, do.osa=F, do.retro=F)  }  #save the model fits for(m in 1:length(mods)) saveRDS(mod[[m]], file=paste0("m",m,".rds"))

title: Ex 4: Selectivity with time- and age-varying random effects
text: Check models converged. Plot output models converged (subfolder model): Compare models using AIC: Prepare plot selectivity--age block 1 (fleet). Now plot selectivity--age block 1 (fleet) models.
code: vign4_conv <- lapply(mods, function(x) capture.output(check_convergence(x))) for(m in 1:n.mods) cat(paste0("Model ",m,":"), vign4_conv[[m]], "", sep='\n') #> Model 1: #> stats:nlminb thinks the model has converged: mod$opt$convergence == 0 #> Maximum gradient component: 6.24e-11  #> Max gradient parameter: log_NAA_sigma  #> TMB:sdreport() was performed successfully for this model #>  #> Model 2: #> stats:nlminb thinks the model has converged: mod$opt$convergence == 0 #> Maximum gradient component: 8.81e-13  #> Max gradient parameter: logit_q  #> TMB:sdreport() was performed successfully for this model #>  #> Model 3: #> stats:nlminb thinks the model has converged: mod$opt$convergence == 0 #> Maximum gradient component: 2.84e-13  #> Max gradient parameter: logit_q  #> TMB:sdreport() was performed successfully for this model #>  #> Model 4: #> stats:nlminb thinks the model has converged: mod$opt$convergence == 0 #> Maximum gradient component: 1.71e-10  #> Max gradient parameter: log_NAA_sigma  #> TMB:sdreport() was performed successfully for this model #>  #> Model 5: #> stats:nlminb thinks the model has converged: mod$opt$convergence == 0 #> Maximum gradient component: 5.27e-08  #> Max gradient parameter: catch_paa_pars  #> TMB:sdreport() was performed successfully for this model #>  #> Model 6: #> stats:nlminb thinks the model has converged: mod$opt$convergence == 0 #> Maximum gradient component: 5.61e-13  #> Max gradient parameter: logit_q  #> TMB:sdreport() was performed successfully for this model #>  #> Model 7: #> stats:nlminb thinks the model has converged: mod$opt$convergence == 0 #> Maximum gradient component: 1.08e-09  #> Max gradient parameter: index_paa_pars  #> TMB:sdreport() was performed successfully for this model #>  #> Model 8: #> stats:nlminb thinks the model has converged: mod$opt$convergence == 0 #> Maximum gradient component: 3.58e-09  #> Max gradient parameter: F_pars  #> TMB:sdreport() was performed successfully for this model # Is Hessian positive definite? ok_sdrep = sapply(mods, function(x) if(x$na_sdrep==FALSE & !is.na(x$na_sdrep)) 1 else 0) pdHess <- as.logical(ok_sdrep) # did stats::nlminb converge? conv <- sapply(mods, function(x) x$opt$convergence == 0) # 0 means opt converged conv_mods <- (1:n.mods)[pdHess]  for(m in conv_mods){     #html and png files by default     plot_wham_output(mod=mods[[m]], dir.main=file.path(write.dir,paste0("m",m))) } df.aic <- as.data.frame(compare_wham_models(mods, table.opts=list(sort=FALSE, calc.rho=FALSE))$tab) df.aic[!pdHess,] = NA minAIC <- min(df.aic$AIC, na.rm=T) df.aic$dAIC <- round(df.aic$AIC - minAIC,1) df.mods <- cbind(data.frame(Model=paste0("m",1:n.mods), Selectivity=sel_model,                             "Block1_re"=sapply(sel_re, function(x) x[[1]]),                             "opt_converged"= ifelse(conv, "Yes", "No"),                             "pd_hessian"= ifelse(pdHess, "Yes", "No"),                             "NLL"=sapply(mods, function(x) round(x$opt$objective,3)),                             "Runtime"=sapply(mods, function(x) x$runtime)), df.aic) rownames(df.mods) <- NULL df.mods #>   Model  Selectivity Block1_re opt_converged pd_hessian      NLL Runtime dAIC #> 1    m1     logistic      none           Yes        Yes -788.748    0.73 54.5 #> 2    m2     logistic       iid           Yes        Yes -811.603    0.87 10.8 #> 3    m3     logistic     2dar1           Yes        Yes -818.980    0.89  0.0 #> 4    m4 age-specific      none           Yes        Yes -793.497    0.67 51.0 #> 5    m5 age-specific       iid           Yes        Yes -793.497    0.84 53.0 #> 6    m6 age-specific       ar1           Yes        Yes -785.042    0.85 67.9 #> 7    m7 age-specific     ar1_y           Yes        Yes -818.610    0.68  4.8 #> 8    m8 age-specific     2dar1           Yes        Yes -820.487    0.84  3.0 #>       AIC #> 1 -1449.5 #> 2 -1493.2 #> 3 -1504.0 #> 4 -1453.0 #> 5 -1451.0 #> 6 -1436.1 #> 7 -1499.2 #> 8 -1501.0 library(tidyverse) library(viridis)  selAA <- lapply(mods, function(x) x$report()$selAA[[1]]) sel_mod <- factor(c("Age-specific","Logistic")[sapply(mods, function(x) x$env$data$selblock_models[1])], levels=c("Logistic","Age-specific")) sel_cor <- factor(c("None","IID","AR1","AR1_y","2D AR1")[sapply(mods, function(x) x$env$data$selblock_models_re[1])], levels=c("None","IID","AR1","AR1_y","2D AR1")) df.selAA <- data.frame(matrix(NA, nrow=0, ncol=11)) colnames(df.selAA) <- c(paste0("Age_",1:6),"Year","Model","conv","sel_mod","sel_cor") for(m in 1:n.mods){     df <- as.data.frame(selAA[[m]])     df$Year <- input$years     colnames(df) <- c(paste0("Age_",1:6),"Year")     df$Model <- m     df$conv <- ifelse(df.mods$pd_hessian[m]=="Yes",1,0)     df$sel_mod = sel_mod[m]     df$sel_cor = sel_cor[m]     df.selAA <- rbind(df.selAA, df) } df <- df.selAA |> pivot_longer(-c(Year,Model,conv,sel_mod,sel_cor),                 names_to = "Age",                 names_prefix = "Age_",                 names_transform = list(Age = as.integer),                 values_to = "Selectivity") df$Age <- as.integer(df$Age) df$sel_mod <- factor(df$sel_mod, levels=c("Logistic","Age-specific")) df$sel_cor <- factor(df$sel_cor, levels=c("None","IID","AR1","AR1_y","2D AR1")) df$conv = factor(df$conv) print(ggplot(df, aes(x=Year, y=Age)) +     geom_tile(aes(fill=Selectivity)) +     geom_label(aes(x=Year, y=Age, label=lab), size=5, alpha=1, #fontface = "bold",       data=data.frame(Year=1975.5, Age=5.8, lab=paste0("m",1:length(mods)), sel_mod=sel_mod, sel_cor=sel_cor)) +         facet_grid(rows=vars(sel_cor), cols=vars(sel_mod)) +     scale_fill_viridis() +     theme_bw() +     scale_x_continuous(expand=c(0,0)) +     scale_y_continuous(expand=c(0,0)))

title: Ex 4: Selectivity with time- and age-varying random effects
text: fitting age-specific selectivity, oftentimes (mean, μas\mu^s_a) selectivity parameters need fixed model converge. specifications used follow procedure: Fit model without fixing selectivity parameters. model fails converge hessian invertible (.e. positive definite), look mean selectivity parameters close 0 1 (> 5 < -5 logit scale) /NaN estimates standard error: Re-run model fixing worst selectivity--age parameter block 0 1 appropriate. age-specific models, initially just fixed age 4 block 2. logit scale selectivity age 5 block around 20 least one model, indicating fixed 1. Sometimes just initializing worst parameter enough, without fixing . goal find set selectivity parameter initial/fixed values allow nested models converge. Fixing parameters affect NLL much, model superset another greater NLL (indicates converged global minimum). following commands may helpful:
code: mod$parList$logit_selpars # mean sel pars mod$input$map$sel_repars # if time-varying selectivity turned on mod$rep$selAA # list of annual selectivity-at-age by block mod$sdrep # look for sel pars with NaN standard errors mod.list <- file.path(getwd(),paste0("m",1:n.mods,".rds")) mods <- lapply(mod.list, readRDS) sapply(mods, function(x) check_convergence(x)) sapply(mods, function(x) x$opt$obj) # get NLL  lapply(mods, function(x) x$parList$logit_selpars) lapply(mods, function(x) x$input$map$sel_repars)

title: Ex 5: Time-varying natural mortality linked to the Gulf Stream Index
text: Open R load wham useful packages: clean, runnable .R script, look ex5_M_GSI.R example_scripts folder wham package. can run entire example script : Let’s create directory analysis: need ASAP data file example 2, environmental covariate (Gulf Stream Index, GSI). Read ex2_SNEMAYT.dat GSI.csv: GSI standard error estimate, either yearly observation one overall value. case, WHAM can estimate observation error environmental covariate, either one overall value, σE\sigma_E, yearly values random effects, logσEy∼𝒩(logσE,σσE2)\mathrm{log}\sigma_{E_y} \sim \mathcal{N}(\mathrm{log}\sigma_E, \sigma^2_{\sigma_E}). example choose simpler option estimate one observation error parameter, shared across years.
code: library(wham) library(ggplot2) library(tidyr) library(dplyr) library(viridis) wham.dir <- find.package("wham") source(file.path(wham.dir, "example_scripts", "ex5_M_GSI.R")) # choose a location to save output, otherwise will be saved in working directory write.dir <- "choose/where/to/save/output" # need to change e.g., tempdir(check=TRUE) dir.create(write.dir) setwd(write.dir) asap3 <- read_asap3_dat(file.path(wham.dir,"extdata","ex2_SNEMAYT.dat")) env.dat <- read.csv(file.path(wham.dir,"extdata","GSI.csv"), header=T) head(env.dat)

title: Ex 5: Time-varying natural mortality linked to the Gulf Stream Index
text: Now specify 14 models different options natural mortality: Look model table: first 6 models fix mean natural mortality rates. models assume age year varying random effects effects GSI M. Model 7 estimates age-specific M fixed effects Model 8 estimates M function weight age. Models 9-14 make similar assumptions models 1-6, constant mean M parameter (across age time) estimated.
code: Ecov_how <- paste0(   c("none",rep("",2), rep("none", 9), rep("", 2)),   c("", rep("lag-0-",2), rep("",9), rep("lag-0-",2)),   c("", "linear", "poly-2", rep("",9), "linear", "poly-2"))  mean_model <- c(rep("fixed-M",6), "estimate-M", "weight-at-age", rep("estimate-M",6)) age_specific <- c(rep(NA,6),TRUE, NA, rep(FALSE, 6))  df.mods <- data.frame(M_model = c(rep("---",6),"age-specific","weight-at-age",rep("constant",6)),                       mean_model = mean_model,                       age_specific = age_specific,                       M_re = c(rep("none",3),"ar1_a","ar1_y","ar1_ay",rep("none",3),"ar1_a", "ar1_y",rep("ar1_ay",3)),                       Ecov_process = rep("ar1",14),                       Ecov_how = Ecov_how, stringsAsFactors=FALSE) n.mods <- dim(df.mods)[1] df.mods$Model <- paste0("m",1:n.mods) df.mods <- df.mods |> select(Model, everything()) # moves Model to first col df.mods #>    Model       M_model    mean_model age_specific   M_re Ecov_process #> 1     m1           ---       fixed-M           NA   none          ar1 #> 2     m2           ---       fixed-M           NA   none          ar1 #> 3     m3           ---       fixed-M           NA   none          ar1 #> 4     m4           ---       fixed-M           NA  ar1_a          ar1 #> 5     m5           ---       fixed-M           NA  ar1_y          ar1 #> 6     m6           ---       fixed-M           NA ar1_ay          ar1 #> 7     m7  age-specific    estimate-M         TRUE   none          ar1 #> 8     m8 weight-at-age weight-at-age           NA   none          ar1 #> 9     m9      constant    estimate-M        FALSE   none          ar1 #> 10   m10      constant    estimate-M        FALSE  ar1_a          ar1 #> 11   m11      constant    estimate-M        FALSE  ar1_y          ar1 #> 12   m12      constant    estimate-M        FALSE ar1_ay          ar1 #> 13   m13      constant    estimate-M        FALSE ar1_ay          ar1 #> 14   m14      constant    estimate-M        FALSE ar1_ay          ar1 #>        Ecov_how #> 1          none #> 2  lag-0-linear #> 3  lag-0-poly-2 #> 4          none #> 5          none #> 6          none #> 7          none #> 8          none #> 9          none #> 10         none #> 11         none #> 12         none #> 13 lag-0-linear #> 14 lag-0-poly-2

title: Ex 5: Time-varying natural mortality linked to the Gulf Stream Index
text: specify options modeling natural mortality including optional list argument, M, prepare_wham_input() function (see prepare_wham_input() set_M() help pages). M specifies estimation options can overwrite M--age values specified ASAP data file. default (.e. M NULL included), M--age matrix ASAP data file used (M fixed, estimated). M list includes following entries relevant : $mean_model: Natural mortality model options. "constant": estimate single MM, shared across ages years. "age-specific": estimate MaM_a independent age, shared across years. "weight--age": estimate MM function weight--age, ,=μM*Wy,abM_{y,} = \mu_M * W_{y,}^b, Lorenzen (1996) Miller & Hyun (2018). $re_model: Time- age-varying random effects MM. "none": MM constant time across ages (default). "iid_a": MM varies age, uncorrelated constant years. "iid_y": MM varies year, uncorrelated constant age. "iid_ay": MM varies year age, uncorrelated. "ar1_a": MM correlated age (AR1), constant time. "ar1_y": MM correlated year (AR1), constant age. "ar1_ay": MM correlated year age (2D AR1), Cadigan (2016). $initial_means: array initial/mean M parameters (n_stocks x n_regions x n_ages). NULL, initial mean M--age values taken first row MAA matrix ASAP data file(s). $means_map: array integers (n_stocks x n_regions x n_ages) distinguishes mean parameters estimate whether value. models 1 stock 1 region 6 ages. estimate different M age set: $means_map = array(1:6, dim = c(1,1,6)). NULL, MM ages fixed M$initial_means (NULL) row 1 MAA matrix ASAP file(s) (M$initial_means = NULL). example, fit model m1, fix MaM_a values ASAP file: fit model m9, estimate one MM, constant year age: fit model m12 estimate mean MM parameter 2DAR1 deviations year age: fit model m11, use MaM_a values specified ASAP file, 2D AR1 deviations Cadigan (2016):
code: M <- NULL # or simply leave out of call to prepare_wham_input M <- list(mean_model="estimate-M", means_map = array(1,dim = c(1,1,6))) M <- list(model="estimate-M", means_map = array(1, dim = c(1,1,asap3[[1]]$dat$n_ages)), re_model="ar1_ay") M <- list(re_model=matrix("ar1_ay",1,1))

what: 4. Linking M to an environmental covariate (GSI)
title: Ex 5: Time-varying natural mortality linked to the Gulf Stream Index
text: described example 2, environmental covariate options fed prepare_wham_input() list, ecov. example differs example 2 : estimate observation error GSI (one overall value years): $logsigma = "est_1". option "est_re" allow GSI observation error yearly fluctuations (random effects). Cold Pool Index example 2 yearly observation errors given. assume GSI affects MM rather recruitment. assume lag 0 effect GSI MM: GSI year t affects MM year t, instead year t+1. consider models without effect GSI MM, keep state-space model GSI model allow AIC comparisons. consider linear quadratic effects GSI MM. WHAM includes function calculate orthogonal polynomials TMB, akin poly() function R. example, ecov list model m3 quadratic GSI-M effect: Note can set ecov = NULL fit model without environmental covariate data, fit ecov data even models without GSI effect MM (m1, m4-12) can compare via AIC (need data likelihood). accomplish setting ecov$M_how = array("none",c(1,1,6,1)) ecov$process_model = "ar1".
code: # example for model m3   ecov <- list(     label = "GSI",     mean = as.matrix(env.dat$GSI),     logsigma = 'est_1', # estimate obs sigma, 1 value shared across years     year = env.dat$year,     use_obs = matrix(1, ncol=1, nrow=dim(env.dat)[1]), # use all obs (=1)     lag = 0, # GSI in year t affects M in same year     process_model = "ar1", # GSI modeled as AR1 (random walk would be "rw")     M_how = array("lag-0-poly-2",c(1,1,6,1))) # n_Ecov x n_stocks x n_ages x n_regions

title: Ex 5: Time-varying natural mortality linked to the Gulf Stream Index
text: models use options recruitment (random--mean, stock-recruit function) selectivity (logistic, parameters fixed indices 4 5).
code: mods <- list() for(m in 1:n.mods){   ecov <- list(     label = "GSI",     mean = as.matrix(env.dat$GSI),     logsigma = 'est_1', # estimate obs sigma, 1 value shared across years     year = env.dat$year,     use_obs = matrix(1, ncol=1, nrow=dim(env.dat)[1]), # use all obs (=1)     process_model = df.mods$Ecov_process[m], # "rw" or "ar1"     M_how = array(df.mods$Ecov_how[m],c(1,1,asap3[[1]]$dat$n_ages,1))) # n_Ecov x n_stocks x n_ages x n_regions    mean_map <- NULL   if(df.mods$mean_model[m] == "estimate-M"){     if(df.mods$age_specific[m]) mean_map[1,1,] <- 1:asap3[[1]]$dat$n_ages     else mean_map[1,1,] <- 1   }   M <- list(     mean_model = df.mods$mean_model[m],     re_model = matrix(df.mods$M_re[m], 1,1),     means_map = mean_map   )   if(df.mods$mean_model[m] == "estimate-M" & !df.mods$age_specific[m]) M$initial_means = array(0.28, c(1,1,asap3[[1]]$dat$n_ages)) #n_stocks x n_regions x n_ages    input <- prepare_wham_input(asap3, recruit_model = 2,     model_name = paste0("m",m,": ", df.mods$mean_model[m]," + GSI link: ",df.mods$Ecov_how[m]," + M RE: ", df.mods$M_re[m]),     ecov = ecov,     selectivity=list(model=rep("logistic",6),       initial_pars=c(rep(list(c(3,3)),4), list(c(1.5,0.1), c(1.5,0.1))),       fix_pars=c(rep(list(NULL),4), list(1:2, 1:2))),     NAA_re = list(sigma='rec+1',cor='iid'),     M=M,     age_comp = "logistic-normal-pool0")    # Fit model   mods[[m]] <- fit_wham(input, do.retro=T, do.osa=F) # turn off OSA residuals to save time    # Save model   saveRDS(mod[[m]], file=paste0(df.mods$Model[m],".rds"))    # If desired, plot output in new subfolder   # plot_wham_output(mod=mod, dir.main=file.path(getwd(),df.mods$Model[m]), out.type='html')      # If desired, do projections   # mod_proj <- project_wham(mod)   # saveRDS(mod_proj, file=paste0(df.mods$Model[m],"_proj.rds")) }

title: Ex 5: Time-varying natural mortality linked to the Gulf Stream Index
text: Get model convergence stats. calculate AIC Mohn’s rho converged models. Look results table.
code: opt_conv = 1-sapply(mods, function(x) x$opt$convergence) ok_sdrep = sapply(mods, function(x) if(x$na_sdrep==FALSE & !is.na(x$na_sdrep)) 1 else 0) df.mods$conv <- as.logical(opt_conv) df.mods$pdHess <- as.logical(ok_sdrep) df.mods$runtime <- sapply(mods, function(x) x$runtime) df.mods$NLL <- sapply(mods, function(x) round(x$opt$objective,3)) is_conv <- df.mods$conv & df.mods$pdHess which(is_conv) # 1, 2, 3, 5, 8, 9, 11, 12 mods2 <- mods[is_conv] #mods2[not_conv] <- NULL df.aic.tmp <- as.data.frame(compare_wham_models(mods2, table.opts=list(sort=FALSE, calc.rho=TRUE))$tab) df.aic <- df.aic.tmp[FALSE,] ct = 1 for(i in 1:n.mods){   if(!is_conv[i]){     df.aic[i,] <- rep(NA,5)   } else {     df.aic[i,] <- df.aic.tmp[ct,]     ct <- ct + 1   } } df.aic[,1:2] <- format(round(df.aic[,1:2], 1), nsmall=1) df.aic[,3:5] <- format(round(df.aic[,3:5], 3), nsmall=3) df.aic[grep("NA",df.aic$dAIC),] <- "---" df.mods <- cbind(df.mods, df.aic) rownames(df.mods) <- NULL df.mods

title: Ex 6: Numbers-at-age / survival deviations as random effects
text: Open R load wham useful packages: clean, runnable .R script, look ex6_NAA.R example_scripts folder wham package. can run entire example script : Let’s create directory analysis: need ASAP data file example 1, environmental covariate (Gulf Stream Index, GSI). Read ex1_SNEMAYT.dat GSI.csv: example 5, GSI data file standard error estimate, either yearly observation one overall value. case, WHAM can estimate observation error environmental covariate, either one overall value, σGSI\sigma_{\mathrm{GSI}}, yearly values random effects, logσGSIy∼𝒩(logσGSI,σσGSI2)\mathrm{log}\sigma_{\mathrm{GSI}_y} \sim \mathcal{N}(\mathrm{log}\sigma_{\mathrm{GSI}}, \sigma^2_{\sigma_{\mathrm{GSI}}}). example choose simpler option estimate one observation error parameter, shared across years.
code: library(wham) library(tidyr) library(dplyr) wham.dir <- find.package("wham") source(file.path(wham.dir, "example_scripts", "ex6_NAA.R")) # choose a location to save output, otherwise will be saved in working directory write.dir <- "choose/where/to/save/output" # need to change e.g., tempdir(check=TRUE) dir.create(write.dir) setwd(write.dir) asap3 <- read_asap3_dat(file.path(wham.dir,"extdata","ex2_SNEMAYT.dat")) env.dat <- read.csv(file.path(wham.dir,"extdata","GSI.csv"), header=T) head(env.dat)

title: Ex 6: Numbers-at-age / survival deviations as random effects
text: Now specify several models different options numbers--age (NAA) transitions, .e. survival: Look model table:
code: df.mods <- data.frame(NAA_cor = c('---','iid','ar1_y','iid','ar1_a','ar1_y','2dar1','iid','ar1_y','iid','ar1_a','ar1_y','2dar1'),                       NAA_sigma = c('---',rep("rec",2),rep("rec+1",4),rep("rec",2),rep("rec+1",4)),                       R_how = paste0(c(rep("none",7),rep("limiting-lag-1-linear",6))), stringsAsFactors=FALSE) n.mods <- dim(df.mods)[1] df.mods$Model <- paste0("m",1:n.mods) df.mods <- df.mods |> select(Model, everything()) # moves Model to first col df.mods #>    Model NAA_cor NAA_sigma                 R_how #> 1     m1     ---       ---                  none #> 2     m2     iid       rec                  none #> 3     m3   ar1_y       rec                  none #> 4     m4     iid     rec+1                  none #> 5     m5   ar1_a     rec+1                  none #> 6     m6   ar1_y     rec+1                  none #> 7     m7   2dar1     rec+1                  none #> 8     m8     iid       rec limiting-lag-1-linear #> 9     m9   ar1_y       rec limiting-lag-1-linear #> 10   m10     iid     rec+1 limiting-lag-1-linear #> 11   m11   ar1_a     rec+1 limiting-lag-1-linear #> 12   m12   ar1_y     rec+1 limiting-lag-1-linear #> 13   m13   2dar1     rec+1 limiting-lag-1-linear

title: Ex 6: Numbers-at-age / survival deviations as random effects
text: specify options modeling NAA transitions, include optional list argument, NAA_re, prepare_wham_input function (see prepare_wham_input help page). ASAP3 estimate random effects, therefore options specified ASAP data file. default (NAA_re NULL included), WHAM fits traditional statistical catch--age model (NAA = predicted NAA ages, .e. survival deterministic). fit state-space model, must specify NAA_re. NAA_re list following entries: "rec": Recruitment deviations random effects, survival ages deterministic "rec+1": Survival ages stochastic (“full state space model”), 2 estimated σa\sigma_a, one recruitment one shared among ages "iid": NAA deviations vary year age, uncorrelated. "ar1_a": NAA deviations correlated age (AR1). "ar1_y": NAA deviations correlated year (AR1). "2dar1": NAA deviations correlated year age (2D AR1, MM example 5). Alternatively, can specify complex configuration sigma parameter estimation via NAA_re$sigma_map array (n_stocks x n_regions x n_ages) integers (NAs fix parameters). example (1 stock 1 region ), NAA_re$sigma = array(c(1,2,2,3,3,3), dim = c(1,1,6)) estimate three σ\sigma parameters, recruitment (age-1) deviations σR\sigma_R, ages 2-3 sharing σ2\sigma_2, ages 4-6 sharing σ3\sigma_3. fit model m1 (SCAA) supply anything: fit model m3, recruitment deviations correlated random effects: fit model m7, numbers ages random effects correlated year age:
code: NAA_re <- NULL # or simply leave out of call to prepare_wham_input NAA_re <- list(sigma="rec", cor="ar1_y") NAA_re <- list(sigma="rec+1", cor="2dar1")

what: 4. Linking recruitment to an environmental covariate (GSI)
title: Ex 6: Numbers-at-age / survival deviations as random effects
text: described example 2, environmental covariate options fed prepare_wham_input list, ecov. example differs example 2 : ecov$logsigma = "est_1" estimates GSI observation error (σGSI\sigma_{\mathrm{GSI}}, one overall value years like example 5). option "est_re" allow GSI observation error yearly fluctuations (random effects). Cold Pool Index example 2 yearly observation errors given, necessary. ecov$R_how = matrix("none",1,1) ecov$R_how = NULL estimates GSI time-series model (AR1) models without GSI-Recruitment effect, order compare AIC models include effect. Setting ecov$R_how = matrix("limiting-lag-1-linear,1,1) specifies GSI year tt affects Beverton-Holt β\beta parameter (“limiting” / carrying capacity effect) year t+1t+1 linearly (log scale). example, ecov list models m8-m13 linear GSI-β\beta effect: Note can set ecov = NULL fit model without environmental covariate data, fit ecov data even models without GSI effect recruitment (m1-m7) can compare via AIC (need data likelihood). accomplish setting ecov$R_how = matrix("none",1,1) ecov$process_model = "ar1".
code: ecov <- list(     label = "GSI",     mean = as.matrix(env.dat$GSI),     logsigma = 'est_1', # estimate obs sigma, 1 value shared across years     year = env.dat$year,     use_obs = matrix(1, ncol=1, nrow=dim(env.dat)[1]), # use all obs (=1)     process_model = 'ar1', # "rw" or "ar1"     R_how = matrix("limiting-lag-1-linear",1,1)) # n_Ecov x n_stocks x n_ages x n_regions

title: Ex 6: Numbers-at-age / survival deviations as random effects
text: models use options expected recruitment (Beverton-Holt stock-recruit function) selectivity (age-specific, one two ages fixed 1). specify recruitment decoupling consistency original implicit assumption WHAM.
code: mods <- vector("list",n.mods) for(m in 1:n.mods){   NAA_list <- list(cor=df.mods[m,"NAA_cor"], sigma=df.mods[m,"NAA_sigma"], decouple_recruitment = FALSE)   if(NAA_list$sigma == '---') NAA_list = NULL    ecov <- list(     label = "GSI",     mean = as.matrix(env.dat$GSI),     logsigma = 'est_1', # estimate obs sigma, 1 value shared across years     year = env.dat$year,     use_obs = matrix(1, ncol=1, nrow=dim(env.dat)[1]), # use all obs (=1)     process_model = 'ar1', # "rw" or "ar1"     recruitment_how = matrix(df.mods$R_how[m])) # n_Ecov x n_stocks    input <- suppressWarnings(prepare_wham_input(asap3, recruit_model = 3, # Bev Holt recruitment                               model_name = "Ex 6: Numbers-at-age",                               selectivity=list(model=rep("age-specific",3), re=c("none","none","none"),                                  initial_pars=list(c(0.1,0.5,0.5,1,1,1),c(0.5,0.5,0.5,1,0.5,0.5),c(0.5,0.5,1,1,1,1)),                                  fix_pars=list(4:6,4,3:6)),                               NAA_re = NAA_list,                               ecov=ecov,                               age_comp = "logistic-normal-miss0")) # logistic normal, treat 0 obs as missing    # Fit model   mods[[m]] <- fit_wham(input, do.retro=T, do.osa=F)    # Save model   saveRDS(mods[[m]], file=paste0(df.mods$Model[m],".rds"))    # If desired, do projections   # mod_proj <- project_wham(mod)   # saveRDS(mod_proj, file=paste0(df.mods$Model[m],"_proj.rds")) }

title: Ex 6: Numbers-at-age / survival deviations as random effects
text: Get model convergence stats. calculate AIC Mohn’s rho converged models. Look results table. Save results table. Plot output models converged.
code: opt_conv = 1-sapply(mods, function(x) x$opt$convergence) ok_sdrep = sapply(mods, function(x) if(x$na_sdrep==FALSE & !is.na(x$na_sdrep)) 1 else 0) df.mods$conv <- as.logical(opt_conv) df.mods$pdHess <- as.logical(ok_sdrep) df.mods$runtime <- sapply(mods, function(x) x$runtime) df.mods$NLL <- sapply(mods, function(x) round(x$opt$objective,3)) not_conv <- !df.mods$conv | !df.mods$pdHess mods2 <- mods mods2[not_conv] <- NULL df.aic.tmp <- as.data.frame(compare_wham_models(mods2, table.opts=list(sort=FALSE, calc.rho=TRUE))$tab) df.aic <- df.aic.tmp[FALSE,] ct = 1 for(i in 1:n.mods){   if(not_conv[i]){     df.aic[i,] <- rep(NA,5)   } else {     df.aic[i,] <- df.aic.tmp[ct,]     ct <- ct + 1   } } df.aic[,1:2] <- format(round(df.aic[,1:2], 1), nsmall=1) df.aic[,3:5] <- format(round(df.aic[,3:5], 3), nsmall=3) df.aic[grep("NA",df.aic$dAIC),] <- "---" df.mods <- cbind(df.mods, df.aic) rownames(df.mods) <- NULL df.mods write.csv(df.mods, file="ex6_table.csv",quote=F, row.names=F) mods[[1]]$env$data$recruit_model = 2 # m1 (SCAA) didn't actually fit a Bev-Holt for(m in which(!not_conv)){   plot_wham_output(mod=mods[[m]], dir.main=file.path(getwd(),paste0("m",m))) #html by default }

title: Ex 6: Numbers-at-age / survival deviations as random effects
text: Two models similar AIC overwhelmingly supported relative models (bold table ): m11 (NAA random effects correlation age, GSI-Recruitment effect) m13 (NAA random effects correlation age year, GSI-Recruitment effect). SCAA state-space models independent NAA deviations lowest runtime. Estimating NAA deviations age-1, .e. recruitment random effects, broke Hessian sparseness, making models m2, m3, m8, m9 slowest. Adding correlation structure NAA deviations increased runtime roughly 50% (comparing models m4 m5-m7 m10 m11-m13). models except m3 converged successfully inverted Hessian produce SE estimates (fixed effect) parameters. Inspection fixed effects parameter estimates shows 2 logit transformed selectivity parameters large implying selectivity 1 ages. Fixing parameters 1 probably correct issue. WHAM stores information hessian invertibility mod$na_sdrep (FALSE), mod$sdrep$pdHess (TRUE). Also, mod$opt$convergence generally 0. See stats::nlminb() TMB::sdreport() details. AIC model m1 comparable models comparing models parameters (, recruitment deviations) estimated fixed effects versus random effects messy marginal AIC appropriate. Still, include m1 show 1) WHAM can fit NAA option, 2) poor retrospective pattern status quo assessment. Note m2 identical m1 except recruitment deviations random effects instead fixed effects. retrospective patterns similar, m2 takes 3x longer run.

title: Ex 8: Compare ASAP and WHAM model results
text: models use options expected recruitment (random mean, stock-recruit function) selectivity specified BASE_3.DAT.
code: for(m in 1:n.mods){   NAA_list <- list(cor=df.mods[m,"naa_cor"], sigma=df.mods[m,"naa_sig"])   if(NAA_list$sigma == 'none') NAA_list = NULL    input <- prepare_wham_input(asap3, recruit_model = 2,                               model_name = df.mods$Model[m],                                                        NAA_re = NAA_list)       mod <- fit_wham(input, do.osa=F)   saveRDS(mod, file=file.path(write.dir, paste0(df.mods$Model[m],".rds"))) }

title: Ex 8: Compare ASAP and WHAM model results
text: Get output ASAP model run using read_asap3_fit(). combine ASAP model 3 WHAM models named list, mods.
code: mod.list <- file.path(write.dir,paste0("m",1:n.mods,".rds")) mods <- lapply(mod.list, readRDS) ok_sdrep = sapply(mods, function(x) if(x$na_sdrep==FALSE & !is.na(x$na_sdrep)) 1 else 0) df.mods$conv <- sapply(mods, function(x) x$opt$convergence == 0) # 0 means opt converged df.mods$pdHess <- as.logical(ok_sdrep) conv_mods <- (1:n.mods)[df.mods$pdHess]  for(m in conv_mods){   plot_wham_output(mod=mods[[m]], out.type='pdf', dir.main=file.path(write.dir,paste0("m",m))) } base <- read_asap3_fit(wd=asap.dir, asap.name="BASE_3") mods <- c(list(base),mods) names(mods) <- c("ASAP",paste0("WHAM-",df.mods$Model))

title: Ex 8: Compare ASAP and WHAM model results
text: Now can use compare_wham_models() plot key output 4 models together comparison. many options, see ?compare_wham_models. get AIC retro table, plots (note ASAP models included ). get plots, table
code: res <- compare_wham_models(mods, fdir=write.dir, plot.opts=list(kobe.prob=FALSE)) saveRDS(res, file=file.path(write.dir,"res.rds")) res <- compare_wham_models(mods, fdir=write.dir, do.plot=F) round(res$tab,2) #>          dAIC    AIC rho_R rho_SSB rho_Fbar #> WHAM-m1   0.0 5749.1  0.89    0.29    -0.24 #> WHAM-m3 123.1 5872.2  0.40    0.21    -0.20 #> WHAM-m2 354.9 6104.0  0.79    0.28    -0.24 res <- compare_wham_models(mods, fdir=write.dir, do.table=F)

what: Ex 8: Compare ASAP and WHAM model results
title: Ex 8: Compare ASAP and WHAM model results
text: Plots 6 7 interesting M time-varying, omit .

title: Ex 8: Compare ASAP and WHAM model results
text: Many modifications can made using built-options, see $plot.opts compare_wham_models(). Plots saved png default, can pdf $lets choose plots make $years lets plot subset model years  $ci can turn confidence interval shading models  $relative.lets plot differences models relative base model (, ASAP)  $kobe.yr used specify year Kobe relative status plot $kobe.prob = F turn probabilities printed quadrant model (can crowded many models)  plots made ggplot2 (except Kobe) returned list, $g, can modify later. example, re-make relative status timeseries plot different fill color scales:  Note even ’re making one plot using $, res$g still list length 10. example, plot SSB, F, recruitment 1980-2018 relative ASAP without confidence intervals:  aesthetics weren’t original plot complicated. example, want make base model line dashed, linetype original plot. can :  final example, changing labels selectivity plot facets  Hopefully enough get started!
code: res <- compare_wham_models(mods, fdir=write.dir, plot.opts=list(out.type='pdf')) # which = 9 (only plot relative status) # years = 1980-2018 compare_wham_models(mods, do.table=F, plot.opts=list(years=1980:2018, which=9)) ggsave(file.path(write.dir,"which9_zoom.png"), device='png', width=6.5, height=5.5, units='in') # which = 1 (SSB, F, recruitment) # ci = FALSE (remove confidence intervals for all models, can also choose a subset) compare_wham_models(mods, fdir=write.dir, do.table=F, plot.opts=list(ci=FALSE, which=1)) ggsave(file.path(write.dir,"which1_noCI.png"), device='png', width=6.5, height=5.5, units='in') # which = 2 (CV of SSB, F, recruitment) # relative to ASAP compare_wham_models(mods, fdir=write.dir, do.table=F, plot.opts=list(ci=FALSE, relative.to="ASAP", which=2)) ggsave(file.path(write.dir,"which2_relative.png"), device='png', width=6.5, height=5.5, units='in') # which = 10 (kobe plot) # kobe.yr = 2010 (instead of terminal year, 2018) # kobe.prob = F (don't print probabilities) compare_wham_models(mods, fdir=write.dir, do.table=F, plot.opts=list(which=10, kobe.yr=2010, kobe.prob=F)) res$g[[9]] + scale_colour_brewer(palette="Set1") + scale_fill_brewer(palette="Set1") ggsave(file.path(write.dir,"which9_colorchange.png"), device='png', width=6.5, height=5.5, units='in') res <- compare_wham_models(mods, do.table=F, plot.opts=list(years=1980:2018, ci=FALSE, relative.to="ASAP", which=1)) cols <- c("black", RColorBrewer::brewer.pal(n = 3, name = "Set1")) res$g[[1]] + scale_colour_manual(values=cols) ggsave(file.path(write.dir,"which1_relative_colorchange.png"), device='png', width=5, height=5.5, units='in') res$g[[1]]$mapping$linetype = quote(Model) res$g[[1]]$labels$linetype = "Model" ltys <- c(2,1,1,1) res$g[[1]] + scale_colour_manual(values=cols) + scale_linetype_manual(values=ltys) ggsave(file.path(write.dir,"which1_relative_colorchange_linetype.png"), device='png', width=5, height=5.5, units='in') res <- compare_wham_models(mods, do.table=F, plot.opts=list(which=4)) index_names <- as_labeller(c(`Block 4` = "NEFSC - Spring", `Block 5` = "NEFSC - Fall",`Block 6` = "DFO", `Block 7` = "NEFSC - Spring41")) res$g[[4]] + facet_wrap(vars(Block), ncol=1, strip.position = 'right', labeller = index_names) ggsave(file.path(write.dir,"which4_labels.png"), device='png', width=4.5, height=5.5, units='in')

what: 1. Load WHAM and other useful packages
title: Ex 9: Retrospective predictions
text: Create directory analysis: need data files example 2. Let’s copy ex2_SNEMAYT.dat CPI.csv analysis directory:
code: # choose a location to save output, otherwise will be saved in working directory write.dir <- "choose/where/to/save/output" dir.create(write.dir) setwd(write.dir) wham.dir <- find.package("wham") file.copy(from=file.path(wham.dir,"extdata","ex2_SNEMAYT.dat"), to=write.dir, overwrite=TRUE) file.copy(from=file.path(wham.dir,"extdata","CPI.csv"), to=write.dir, overwrite=TRUE) asap3 <- read_asap3_dat("ex2_SNEMAYT.dat") env.dat <- read.csv("CPI.csv", header=T)

title: Ex 9: Retrospective predictions
text: Setup models m5 (CPI effect recruitment) m3 (CPI effect recruitment) example 2: full state-space model (numbers--age random effects ages, NAA_re = list(sigma='rec+1',cor='iid')) logistic normal age compositions (age_comp = "logistic-normal-pool0") Beverton-Holt recruitment (recruit_model = 3) Cold Pool Index (CPI) fit AR1 process (ecov$process_model = "ar1") CPI “limiting” (e.g. carrying capacity, Iles Beverton (1998)) effect recruitment (ecov$= "recruit", ecov$= 2)
code: env <- list(   label = "CPI",   mean = as.matrix(env.dat$CPI), # CPI observations   logsigma = as.matrix(log(env.dat$CPI_sigma)), # CPI standard error is given/fixed as data   year = env.dat$Year,   use_obs = matrix(1, ncol=1, nrow=dim(env.dat)[1]), # use all obs (=1)   lag = 1, # CPI in year t affects recruitment in year t+1   process_model = "ar1", # fit CPI as AR1 process   where = "recruit", # CPI affects recruitment   how = NA) # fill in by model in loop  # 2 models, with and without CPI effect on recruitment (both fit CPI data to compare AIC) # Model  Recruit_mod  Ecov_mod     Ecov_how #    m1     Bev-Holt       ar1        --- #    m2     Bev-Holt       ar1     Limiting df.mods <- data.frame(Model = c("m1","m2"), Ecov_how = c(0,2), stringsAsFactors=FALSE) n.mods <- dim(df.mods)[1] df.mods

title: Ex 9: Retrospective predictions
code: for(m in 1:n.mods){     env$how = df.mods$Ecov_how[m]     input <- prepare_wham_input(asap3, recruit_model = 3,                                 model_name = df.mods$Model[m],                                 ecov = env,                                 NAA_re = list(sigma="rec+1", cor="iid"),                                 age_comp = "logistic-normal-pool0") # logistic normal pool 0 obs     input$par$logit_selpars[1:4,7:8] <- 0      # fit model     mod <- fit_wham(input, do.retro=F, do.osa=F)     saveRDS(mod, file=paste0(df.mods$Model[m],".rds"))     plot_wham_output(mod=mod, dir.main=file.path(getwd(),df.mods$Model[m]), out.type='png') }

title: Ex 9: Retrospective predictions
text: Compare model output - pretty similar.
code: mod.list <- file.path(write.dir,paste0("m",1:n.mods,".rds")) mods <- lapply(mod.list, readRDS) ok_sdrep = sapply(mods, function(x) if(x$na_sdrep==FALSE & !is.na(x$na_sdrep)) 1 else 0) df.mods$conv <- sapply(mods, function(x) x$opt$convergence == 0) # 0 means opt converged df.mods$pdHess <- as.logical(ok_sdrep) names(mods) <- c("m1 (no CPI)","m2 (CPI)") res <- compare_wham_models(mods, fdir=write.dir, do.table=F, plot.opts=list(kobe.prob=FALSE))

title: Ex 9: Retrospective predictions
text: model, peel 3-15 years data re-fit model using retro(), project peel 3 years calling project_wham(). projections done F = 0 continuing CPI AR1 process model (without new observations). can specify alternative options projections using $proj.opts (see project_wham() Ex 3: Projections).
code: n.yrs.peel <- 15 n.yrs.proj <- 3 for(m in 1:n.mods){     mods[[m]]$peels <- retro(mods[[m]], ran = unique(names(mods[[m]]$env$par[mods[[m]]$env$random])), n.peels=n.yrs.peel, save.input = T)     for(p in 3:n.yrs.peel) mods[[m]]$peels[[p]] <- project_wham(mods[[m]]$peels[[p]], proj.opts = list(n.yrs = n.yrs.proj, proj.F=rep(0.001,n.yrs.proj))) }

title: Ex 9: Retrospective predictions
text: function extract plot retrospective predictions recruitment model. can easily modified extract variables interest, e.g. SSB F. two black lines represent “true” recruitment model, .e., recruitment estimates fitting full data. Points lines colored peel yellow (1996) dark blue (2008). points terminal year recruitment estimates lines projected recruitment (3 years).
code: plot_retro_pred_R <- function(mods, peels=3:n.yrs.peel, n.yrs.proj=n.yrs.proj){     df <- data.frame(matrix(NA, nrow=0, ncol=6))     colnames(df) <- c("Year","Model","Peel","Recruitment","termyr")     for(m in 1:length(mods)){         for(p in peels){             tmp <- read_wham_fit(mods[[m]]$peels[[p]])             df <- rbind(df, data.frame(Year=tail(tmp$years_full, n.yrs.proj+1),                                         Model=names(mods)[m],                                         Peel=p,                                         Recruitment=tail(exp(tmp$log_NAA[,1]), n.yrs.proj+1),                                         termyr=c(1,rep(0,n.yrs.proj))))         }         # get full model fit, "peel 0"         tmp <- read_wham_fit(mods[[m]])         df <- rbind(df, data.frame(Year=tmp$years,                                     Model=names(mods)[m],                                     Peel=0,                                     Recruitment=exp(tmp$log_NAA[1:length(tmp$years),1]),                                     termyr=0))     }      df <- filter(df, Year > 1990)     df$Model <- factor(df$Model, levels=names(mods), labels=names(mods))     df$Year <- as.integer(df$Year)      df$Peel <- factor(df$Peel)     dfpts <- filter(df, termyr==1)      cols <- c("black", viridis_pal(option="plasma")(length(levels(df$Peel))-1))     g <- ggplot(df, aes(x=Year, y=Recruitment, linetype=Model, color=Peel, fill=Peel, group=interaction(Model,Peel))) +            geom_line(linewidth=1) +           geom_point(data=dfpts, color='black', size=2, pch=21) +           scale_x_continuous(expand=c(0.01,0.01)) + # breaks=scales::breaks_extended(5)           scale_colour_manual(values=cols) +           scale_fill_manual(values=cols) +           guides(color = "none", fill="none") +           scale_y_continuous(expand=c(0.01,0.01), limits = c(0,NA), labels=wham:::fancy_scientific) +           theme_bw() +           theme(legend.position=c(.9,.9), legend.box.margin = margin(0,0,0,0), legend.margin = margin(0,0,0,0))     return(g) } plot_retro_pred_R(mods, peels=3:n.yrs.peel, n.yrs.proj=n.yrs.proj) ggsave(file.path(write.dir,"retro_pred_R.png"), device='png', width=8, height=5, units='in')
