title: Contributing
text: Thank interest contributing WHAM! welcome simple documentation bug fixes well substantive code contributions, new features. current ideas improving WHAM list. like tackle something larger bug fix, please file Issue discuss make sure working already. reserve master branch stable, tagged releases. devel branch contains unstable tested updates. create additional, temporary branches develop test larger updates merging devel. working temporary branch, semi-regularly pull updates devel deal merge conflicts soon possible. wish contribute, please use NOAA Fisheries Toolbox suggested workflow, summarized . page guidance resources. “fork--branch” workflow follow described detail .

title: Contributing
text: File Issue describing fix/feature planning. Fork wham (create copy GitHub clicking “Fork”, top right wham page) Clone fork (download copy wham can modify locally, click green “Code” button) Add original wham repo upstream can get future changes devel (see ). Checkout devel. Confirm can fetch merge changes original wham repo (see ). Create new branch change meaningful name, e.g. selftest add function simulate data fit model refit. Confirm can run pass automated tests Work feature branch, making changes local wham files. Periodically merge updates upstream devel branch (#6 ). Try maintain current wham coding style. Add documentation, test(s), vignette applicable. feature finished, rerun tests verify pass (#8). Helpful scripts testing debugging inst/contribute/ folder. Confirm branch --date upstream devel. Commit changes, push local branch forked repo (origin, upstream) Submit pull request, following best practices. Clean / delete feature branch fork.
code: git remote add upstream https://github.com/timjmiller/wham.git git checkout devel git fetch upstream git merge upstream/devel git checkout -b <myfeature> # install.packages("here") library(here) wham.source.dir <- here()  # confirm that the .cpp compiles and wham can be loaded devtools::load_all(path = wham.source.dir)  # run all tests devtools::test(pkg = wham.source.dir) git commit -m "informative message" git push origin <myfeature>

title: Woods Hole Assessment Model (WHAM)
text: Woods Hole Assessment Model (WHAM) general age-structured stock assessment framework can configured estimate assessment models range complexity statistical catch--age (SCAA) model annual recruitments fixed effects, state-space, multi-stock, multi-region, age-structured models many parameters can treated time- age-varying process errors /allowing effects environmental covariates. default configuration WHAM estimate traditional SCAA model random effects (see Example 6, Example 8, Example 13) WHAM can estimate process errors random effects : recruitment (Example 1, Example 6, Example 8) transitions numbers--age time (Example 1, Example 6, Example 8, Example 13, selectivity (Example 4), natural mortality (Example 5), index catchability (Example 11), movement stocks regions (Example 12) WHAM can also include explicit environmental effects parameters (except selectivity) uses state-space treatment covariates observation error covariates considered (e.g., Example 2 Example 5) nice property treating population environmental processes random effects uncertainty naturally propagated projections/forecasts (Example 3). presentation providing overview earlier version WHAM (Jan 8 2021):

title: Woods Hole Assessment Model (WHAM)
text: WHAM generalizes extends state-space age-structured models Miller et al. (2016), Miller Hyun 2018, Miller et al. 2018. WHAM many similarities ASAP (code, Legault Restrepo 1998), including input data file structure. Many plotting functions input data, results, diagnostics modified code written Chris Legault Liz Brooks (ASAPplots). Stock Miller (2021) describes details model including equations, simulation tests, demos random effects options numbers--age, M, selectivity, environment-recruitment. Stock et al. (2021) describes 2D (year x age) AR(1) correlation structure can used numbers--age, M, selectivity WHAM (Berg Nielsen 2016, Cadigan 2016, Xu et al. 2019). Since , WHAM also extended include covariate random effects index catchability covariate effects M. Miller et al. (2025) provides details latest extension allow multiple regions stocks, possibly covariate random effects movement. Seasonal changes fleet fishing effort movement also possible. version WHAM allows single stock region remains available “single_wham” branch, developed . WHAM R package computation estimation built TMB package (Kristensen et al. 2016), possible without superb open-source tools. information, see: R: https://www.r-project.org/ TMB: https://kaskr.github.io/adcomp/_book/Introduction.html

title: Woods Hole Assessment Model (WHAM)

code: devtools::install_github("timjmiller/wham", dependencies=TRUE) devtools::install_github("timjmiller/wham@devel", dependencies=TRUE) devtools::install_github("timjmiller/wham@single_wham") devtools::install_github("timjmiller/wham@v1.0.9") #or equivalently the associated commit devtools::install_github("timjmiller/wham@40cc14b")

E.g.,
code: pak::pkg_install("timjmiller/wham") pak::pkg_install("timjmiller/wham@devel") pak::pkg_install("timjmiller/wham@single_wham") devtools::install_github("timjmiller/wham", dependencies=TRUE, INSTALL_opts=c("--no-multiarch")) devtools::install_github("timjmiller/wham", dependencies=TRUE, ref = "devel", INSTALL_opts=c("--no-multiarch")) ls() ## no variables search() ## no packages other than Base to.install <- c("plotrix","ellipse","Hmisc","gplots","fields","RColorBrewer","colorspace","mnormt","Deriv","tidyr","dplyr","ggplot2","viridis", "abind", "rmarkdown", "pander", "kableExtra") new.packages <- to.install[!(to.install %in% installed.packages()[,"Package"])] if(length(new.packages)) install.packages(new.packages) install.packages("tinytex") tinytex::install_tinytex() Sys.setenv(RSTUDIO_PANDOC = "path/to/your/pandoc") pak::pkg_install("timjmiller/wham@single_wham", lib = "single_wham") library(wham, lib.loc = "single_wham") .libPaths("single_wham")

Releases lesser changes increment second number (e.g., v2.1.0) releases large changes increment first number (e.g. v3.0.0). commits bug fixes latest release master branch repo increment third number (e.g., v2.0.1). devel branch always --date master branch versioned initially (v).b.c.9000. example, released version changed v2.0.0 v2.0.1, devel branch also go v2.0.0.9xxx v2.0.1.9000. latest pushed commits devel branch fixes released version also unique version. example, next pushed change devel branch v2.0.1.9001.

what: Compare multiple WHAM (or ASAP) models — compare_wham_models
title: Compare multiple WHAM (or ASAP) models — compare_wham_models
text: fitting multiple WHAM (ASAP) models, compare_wham_models produces plots table AIC Mohn's rho aid model comparison.

title: Compare multiple WHAM (or ASAP) models — compare_wham_models
code: compare_wham_models(   mods,   do.table = TRUE,   do.plot = TRUE,   fdir = getwd(),   compare.opts = NULL,   table.opts = NULL,   plot.opts = NULL,   fname = NULL,   sort = NULL,   calc.rho = NULL,   calc.aic = NULL,   do.print = NULL )

title: Compare multiple WHAM (or ASAP) models — compare_wham_models
text: mods (named) list fit WHAM/ASAP models. read ASAP model output, use read_asap3_fit. names given, 'm1', 'm2', ... used. .table T/F, produce table AIC /Mohn's rho? Default = TRUE. .plot T/F, produce plots? Default = TRUE. fdir character, path directory save table /plots. Default = getwd(). compare.opts list options generate comparison results: $stock integer, stock include results. Default = 1. $region integer, region include results. Default = 1. table.opts list options AIC/rho table: $fname character, filename save CSV results table (.csv appended). Default = 'model_comparison'. $sort T/F, sort AIC? Default = TRUE. $calc.rho T/F, calculate Mohn's rho? Retrospective analysis must run modes. Default = TRUE. $calc.aic T/F, calculate AIC? Default = TRUE. $print T/F, print table console? Default = TRUE. $save.csv T/F, save table CSV file? Default = FALSE. plot.opts list options plots: $.type character, either 'pdf' 'png' (default = 'png' sure system('pdftk') work across platforms.) $ci vector T/F, length = 1 (applied models) number models $years vector, years plot? Default = (model projection years). $vector, plots make? Default = . See details. $relative.character, name "base" model plot differences relative . $alpha scalar, (1-alpha)% confidence intervals plotted. Default = 0.05 95% CI. $ages.lab vector, overwrite model age labels. $kobe.yr integer vector (length = 1 (applied models number models, year use Kobe plot (relative status). Default = terminal model year(s). $M.age integer, age use M time-series plot. Default = max(data$which_F_age) (max age F use full total F). $return.ggplot T/F, return list ggplot2 objects later modification? Default = TRUE. $kobe.prob T/F, print probabilities model quadrant Kobe plot? Default = TRUE. $refpt "XSPR" "MSY", reference point use. Default = "XSPR". $browse Open html document web browser ($.type = 'png') (default = TRUE).

title: Compare multiple WHAM (or ASAP) models — compare_wham_models
text: list following components: daic Vector delta-AIC model (.table=T table.opts$calc.aic=T) aic Vector AIC model (.table=T table.opts$calc.aic=T) rho Matrix Mohn's rho model (.table=T table.opts$calc.rho=T) best Name best model (lowest AIC) (.table=T table.opts$calc.aic=T) tab Results table AIC Mohn's rho (.table=T) g List ggplot2 objects later modification (.plot=T plot.opts$return.ggplot=T)

title: Compare multiple WHAM (or ASAP) models — compare_wham_models
text: plot.opts$specifies plots make: 1 3-panel SSB (spawning stock biomass), F (fully-selected fishing mortality), Recruitment 2 CV (coefficient variation) SSB, F, Recruitment 3 Fleet selectivity (block, averaged across years) 4 Index selectivity (block, averaged across years) 5 Selectivity tile (fleets + indices, useful time-varying random effects) 6 M time series (natural mortality, can specify age plot.opts$M.age) 7 M tile (useful time-varying random effects) 8 3-panel F X% SPR, SSB F_X%SPR, yield F_X%SPR 9 2-panel relative status (SSB / SSB F_X%SPR F / F_X%SPR) 10 Kobe status (relative SSB vs. relative F) plot.opts$return.ggplot = TRUE, list g returned holding ggplot2 objects later modification. g[[]] holds plot corresponding , e.g. g[[2]] CV plot.

title: Compare multiple WHAM (or ASAP) models — compare_wham_models

title: Fit TMB model using nlminb — fit_tmb

title: Fit TMB model using nlminb — fit_tmb
text: model Output TMB::MakeADFun. n.newton Integer, number additional Newton steps optimization. Default = 3. .sdrep T/F, calculate standard deviations model parameters? See TMB::sdreport. Default = TRUE. .check T/F, check model parameters identifiable? Runs internal check_estimability, originally provided TMBhelper package. Default = TRUE. save.sdrep T/F, save full TMB::sdreport object? FALSE, save summary.sdreport) reduce model object file size. Default = FALSE. use.optim T/F, use stats::optim instead stats::nlminb? Default = FALSE. opt.control list control parameters pass optimizer. nlminb default = list(iter.max = 1000, eval.max = 1000). optim default = list(maxit=1000).

title: Fit TMB model using nlminb — fit_tmb
text: model, appends following: model$opt Output stats::nlminb model$date System date model$dir Current working directory model$rep model$report(model$env$last.par.best) model$TMB_version Version TMB installed model$parList List parameters, model$env$parList(x = model$opt$par, par = model$env$last.par.best) model$final_gradient Final gradient, model$gr(model$opt$par) model$sdrep Estimated standard deviations model parameters, TMB::sdreport summary.sdreport)

title: Fit WHAM model — fit_wham
text: Fits compiled WHAM model using TMB::MakeADFun stats::nlminb. Runs retrospective analysis specified.

title: Fit WHAM model — fit_wham
code: fit_wham(   input,   n.newton = 3,   do.sdrep = TRUE,   do.retro = TRUE,   n.peels = 7,   do.osa = TRUE,   osa.opts = list(method = "oneStepGaussianOffMode", parallel = TRUE),   do.post.samp = TRUE,   model = NULL,   do.check = FALSE,   MakeADFun.silent = FALSE,   retro.silent = FALSE,   do.proj = FALSE,   proj.opts = list(n.yrs = 3, use.last.F = TRUE, use.avg.F = FALSE, use.FXSPR = FALSE,     proj.F = NULL, proj.catch = NULL, avg.yrs = NULL, cont.ecov = TRUE, use.last.ecov =     FALSE, avg.ecov.yrs = NULL, proj.ecov = NULL, cont.Mre = NULL, avg.rec.yrs = NULL,     percentFXSPR = 100),   do.fit = TRUE,   save.sdrep = TRUE,   do.brps = TRUE,   fit.tmb.control = NULL,   TMB.bias.correct = FALSE,   TMB.jointPrecision = FALSE )

title: Fit WHAM model — fit_wham
text: input Named list components: $data Data fit assessment model . $par Parameters, list parameter objects required user template (random fixed effects). See MakeADFun. $map Map, mechanism collecting fixing parameters. See MakeADFun. $random Character vector defining parameters treat random effect. See MakeADFun. $years Numeric vector years model spans. important model fitting, useful plotting. $model_name Character, name model, e.g. "Yellowtail flounder" $ages.lab Character vector age labels, e.g. c("1","2","3","4+"). n.newton integer, number additional Newton steps optimization. Passed fit_tmb. Default = 3. .sdrep T/F, calculate standard deviations model parameters? See sdreport. Default = TRUE. .retro T/F, retrospective analysis? Default = TRUE. n.peels integer, number peels use retrospective analysis. Default = 7. .osa T/F, calculate one-step-ahead (OSA) residuals? Default = TRUE. See details. Returned mod$osa$residual. osa.opts list 2 options (method, parallel) calculating OSA residuals, passed TMB::oneStepPredict. Default: osa.opts = list(method="oneStepGaussianOffMode", parallel=TRUE). See make_osa_residuals. .post.samp T/F, obtain sample posterior random effects? Default = TRUE. YET IMPLEMENTED. model (optional), previously fit wham model. .check T/F, check model parameters identifiable? Passed fit_tmb. Runs internal function check_estimability, originally provided https://github.com/kaskr/TMB_contrib_R/TMBhelper. Default = TRUE. MakeADFun.silent T/F, Passed silent argument TMB::MakeADFun. Default = FALSE. retro.silent T/F, Passed argument internal retro function. Determines whether peel number printed screen. Default = FALSE. .proj T/F, projections? Default = FALSE. true, runs project_wham. proj.opts named list following components: $n.yrs (integer), number years project/forecast. Default = 3. $use.last.F (T/F), use terminal year F projections. Default = TRUE. $use.FXSPR (T/F), calculate use F X $use.FMSY (T/F), calculate use FMSY projections. $proj.F (vector), user-specified fishing mortality projections. Length must equal n.yrs. $proj.catch (vector), user-specified aggregate catch projections. Length must equal n.yrs. $avg.yrs (vector), specify years average calculating reference points. Default = last 5 model years, tail(model$years, 5). $cont.ecov (T/F), continue ecov process (e.g. random walk AR1) projections. Default = TRUE. $use.last.ecov (T/F), use terminal year ecov projections. $avg.ecov.yrs (vector), specify years average environmental covariate(s) projections. $proj.ecov (matrix), user-specified environmental covariate(s) projections. n.yrs x n_Ecov. $cont.Mre (T/F), continue M random effects (.e. AR1_y 2D AR1) projections. Default = TRUE. FALSE, M averaged $avg.yrs (defaults last 5 model years). $avg.rec.yrs (vector), specify years calculate CDF recruitment use projections. Default = model years. $percentFXSPR (scalar), percent F_XSPR use calculating catch projections, used proj.opts$use.FXSPR = TRUE. example, GOM cod uses F = 75 $percentFMSY (scalar), percent F_MSY use calculating catch projections, used $use.FMSY = TRUE. .fit T/F, fit model using fit_tmb. Default = TRUE. save.sdrep T/F, save full TMB::sdreport object? FALSE, save summary.sdreport reduce model object file size. Default = TRUE. .brps T/F, calculate report biological reference points. Default = TRUE. fit.tmb.control list optimizer controlling attributes passed fit_tmb. Default list(use.optim = FALSE, opt.control = list(iter.max = 1000, eval.max = 1000)), stats::nlminb used opitmize. TMB.bias.correct T/F whether use bias.correct feature TMB::sdreport. Default = FALSE. TMB.jointPrecision T/F whether TMB::sdreport return joint precision matrix fixed random effects. Default = FALSE.

title: Fit WHAM model — fit_wham
text: fit TMB model additional output specified: $rep List derived quantity estimates (see examples) $sdrep Parameter estimates (standard errors .sdrep=TRUE) $peels Retrospective analysis (.retro=TRUE) $osa One-step-ahead residuals (.osa=TRUE)

title: Fit WHAM model — fit_wham
text: Standard residuals appropriate models random effects. Instead, one-step-ahead (OSA) residuals can used evaluating model goodness--fit (Thygeson et al. (2017), implemented TMB::oneStepPredict). Additional OSA residual options passed TMB::oneStepPredict list osa.opts. example, use (much faster, ~1 sec instead 2 min) full Gaussian approximation instead (default) generic method, can use osa.opts=list(method="fullGaussian").

title: Fit WHAM model — fit_wham
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ data("input4_SNEMAYT") # load SNEMA yellowtail flounder data and parameter settings mod = fit_wham(input4_SNEMAYT) # using default values mod = fit_wham(input4_SNEMAYT, do.retro=FALSE, osa.opts=list(method="oneStepGeneric")) # slower OSA method.   names(mod$rep) # list of derived quantities mod$rep$SSB # get SSB estimates (weight, not numbers) m1$rep$NAA[,1] # get recruitment estimates (numbers, first column of numbers-at-age matrix) m1$rep$F[,1] # get F estimates for fleet 1 } # }

what: Prepare input data and parameters to project an already fit WHAM model — prepare_projection
title: Prepare input data and parameters to project an already fit WHAM model — prepare_projection
text: prepare_projection internal function called project_wham, turn called fit_wham .proj = TRUE.

title: Prepare input data and parameters to project an already fit WHAM model — prepare_projection
code: prepare_projection(model, proj.opts, check.version = FALSE)

title: Prepare input data and parameters to project an already fit WHAM model — prepare_projection
text: model previously fit wham model proj.opts named list following components: $n.yrs (integer), number years project/forecast. Default = 3. $use.last.F (T/F), use terminal year F projections. Default = TRUE. $use.avg.F (T/F), use average F certain years projections. Default = FALSE. Years average determined $avg.yrs defined . $use.FXSPR (T/F), calculate use F X% SPR projections. Default = FALSE. $use.FMSY (T/F), calculate use FMSY projections. Default = FALSE. $proj.F (vector), user-specified fishing mortality projections. Length must equal n.yrs. $proj.catch (vector), user-specified aggregate catch projections. Length must equal n.yrs. $avg.yrs (vector), specify years use average population attributes (MAA,FAA,WAA,maturity,movement) projection years. BRPs calculated projection years also use . Default = last 5 years, tail(model$years, 5). $cont.ecov (T/F), continue ecov process (e.g. random walk AR1) projections. Default = TRUE. $use.last.ecov (T/F), use terminal year ecov projections. $avg.ecov.yrs (vector), specify years average environmental covariate(s) projections. $proj.ecov (matrix), user-specified environmental covariate(s) projections. n.yrs x n.ecov. $cont.M.re (T/F), continue M random effects (.e. AR1_y 2D AR1) projections. Default = FALSE. FALSE, M averaged $avg.yrs.M (defaults last 5 model years). $cont.move.re (T/F), continue movement random effects projections. Default = FALSE. FALSE, movement parameters averaged $avg.yrs.move (defaults last 5 model years). $cont.L.re (T/F), continue L ("extra mortality rate") random effects projections. Default = FALSE. FALSE, L parameters averaged $avg.yrs.L (defaults last 5 model years). $avg.rec.yrs (vector), specify years calculate CDF recruitment use projections. Default = model years. used recruitment estimated fixed effects (SCAA). $percentFXSPR (scalar), percent F_XSPR use projections, used $use.FXSPR = TRUE. example, project F = 75% F_40%SPR, proj.opts$percentFXSPR = 75. Default = 100. $percentFMSY (scalar), percent F_MSY use projections, used $use.FMSY = TRUE stock-recruit relationship assumed. Default = 100. $proj_F_opt (integer vector), integers specifying configure year projection: Overrides specifications. 1: Use terminal F. 2: use average F (years determined $avg.yrs). 3: use F X% SPR. 4: user-specified F ($proj_Fcatch). 5: user-specified catch ($proj_Fcatch). 6: use Fmsy (recruit_model>2). $proj_Fcatch (vector matrix), catch F values use projection year: values used using Fmsy, FXSPR, terminal F average F. Overrides specifications proj.F proj.catch. vector, total catch F supplied else matrix columns fleets fleet-specific F found/used (n.yrs x 1 n_fleets). $proj_mature (array), user-supplied maturity values projection years dimensions (n_stocks x n.yrs x n_ages). $proj_waa (3-d array), user-supplied waa values projection years first third dimensions equal model$input$data$waa (waa source x n.yrs x n_ages). $proj_R_opt (integer) specifying recruitment treated projection years 1: Continue RE processes recruitment. 2: Make projected recruitment consistent average recruitment SPR reference points cancel bias correction NAA projection years. 3: Average recruitment deviations $avg.yrs.R ($sigma = "rec"). 4: recruitment deviations ($sigma = "rec"). $proj_NAA_opt (integer) specifying NAA random effects treated projection years $sigma = "rec+1": 1: Continue RE processes NAA. 2: Average NAA deviations $avg.yrs.NAA. 3: NAA deviations. $proj_NAA_init (scalar), default starting value NAA random effects projection years exp(10), may large enough catch specification. Use change default call project_wham suggests . $proj_F_init F initialize internal newton search annual projected F given user-specifed catch. Default 0.1 $avg.yrs.sel list (length = n_fleets), years average selectivity FAA fleet projection years. BRPs calculated projection years also use . Default = last 5 years, tail(model$years, 5). $avg.yrs.waacatch list (length = n_fleets), years average weight age fleet projection years ($proj_waa NULL). BRPs calculated projection years also use . Default = last 5 years, tail(model$years, 5). $avg.yrs.waassb list (length = n_stocks), years average weight age stock SSB projection years ($proj_waa NULL). BRPs calculated projection years also use . Default = last 5 years, tail(model$years, 5). $avg.yrs.mature list (length = n_stocks), years average maturity age stock projection years ($proj_mature NULL). BRPs calculated projection years also use . Default = last 5 years, tail(model$years, 5). $avg.yrs.L list (length = n_regions), years average extra mortality age region projection years ($cond.L.re = FALSE). BRPs calculated projection years also use . Default = last 5 years, tail(model$years, 5). $avg.yrs.M list (length = n_stocks, list length = n_regions), years average natural mortality age stock region projection years ($cont.M.re = FALSE). BRPs calculated projection years also use . Default = last 5 years, tail(model$years, 5). $avg.yrs.move list (length = n_stocks, list length = n_regions), years average movement rates age season stock region (beginning interval) projection years ($cont.move.re = FALSE). BRPs calculated projection years also use . Default = last 5 years, tail(model$years, 5). $avg.yrs.R list (length = n_stocks), years average recruitment deviations stock region projection years ($proj_R_opt = 3). BRPs calculated projection years also use . Default = last 5 years, tail(model$years, 5). $avg.yrs.NAA list (length = n_stocks, list length = n_regions), years average NAA deviations stock region projection years ($proj_NAA_opt = 2). BRPs calculated projection years also use . Default = last 5 years, tail(model$years, 5). check.version T/F check whether version WHAM TMB fitted model match version WHAM using projections. Default = TRUE.

title: Prepare input data and parameters to project an already fit WHAM model — prepare_projection
text: prepare_wham_input, list ready fit_wham: data Named list data, passed TMB::MakeADFun par Named list parameters, passed TMB::MakeADFun map Named list factors determine parameters estimated, passed TMB::MakeADFun random Character vector parameters treat random effects, passed TMB::MakeADFun years Numeric vector representing (non-projection) model years WHAM model years_full Numeric vector representing model projection years WHAM model ages.lab Character vector age labels, ending plus-group model_name Character, name stock/model (specified call prepare_wham_input)

what: Prepare input data and parameters for WHAM model — prepare_wham_input
title: Prepare input data and parameters for WHAM model — prepare_wham_input
text: Prepares data parameter settings  fit_wham, optionally using ASAP3 data file read R read_asap3_dat. default, set SCAA version like ASAP without penalties random effects. asap3, basic_info, catch_info, index_info arguments provided, respective arbitrary assumptions made. various options described , NAA_re selectivity, can still used without asap3 object.

title: Prepare input data and parameters for WHAM model — prepare_wham_input
code: prepare_wham_input(   asap3 = NULL,   model_name = "WHAM for unnamed stock",   recruit_model = 2,   ecov = NULL,   selectivity = NULL,   M = NULL,   NAA_re = NULL,   catchability = NULL,   age_comp = NULL,   move = NULL,   L = NULL,   F = NULL,   catch_info = NULL,   index_info = NULL,   basic_info = NULL )

title: Prepare input data and parameters for WHAM model — prepare_wham_input
text: asap3 (optional) list containing data parameters (output read_asap3_dat) model_name character, name stock/model recruit_model numeric, option specify stock-recruit model (see details) ecov (optional) named list environmental covariate data parameters (see set_ecov full details) selectivity (optional) list specifying selectivity options block: models, initial values, parameters fix, random effects (see set_selectivity full details) M (optional) list specifying natural mortality options: model, random effects, initial values, parameters fix (see set_M full details) NAA_re (optional) list specifying options random effect numbers--age, initial numbers age, recruitment model (see set_NAA full details) catchability (optional) list specifying options priors random effects catchability (see set_q full details) age_comp (optional) character named list, specifies age composition model fleet(s) indices (see set_age_comp full details) move (optional) list specifying movement/migration options models 1 region (see set_move full details) L (optional) list specifying "extra" mortality options (see set_L full details) F (optional) list specifying fishing mortality options (see set_F full details) catch_info (optional) list specifying catch information (see set_catch full details) index_info (optional) list specifying index informaiton (see set_indices full details) basic_info (optional) list basic population information use asap3 provided (see details)

title: Prepare input data and parameters for WHAM model — prepare_wham_input
text: named list following components: data Named list data, passed TMB::MakeADFun par Named list parameters, passed TMB::MakeADFun map Named list defining optionally collect fix parameters, passed TMB::MakeADFun random Character vector parameters treat random effects, passed TMB::MakeADFun years Numeric vector years fit WHAM model (specified ASAP3 .dat file) ages.lab Character vector age labels, ending plus-group (specified ASAP3 .dat file) model_name Character, name stock/model (specified call prepare_wham_input) log list character strings attempting describe input model assumes.

title: Prepare input data and parameters for WHAM model — prepare_wham_input
text: recruit_model specifies stock-recruit model. See wham.cpp implementation. = 1 SCAA (without NAA_re option specified) Random walk (NAA_re$sigma specified), .e. predicted recruitment year = recruitment year -1 = 2 (default) Random effects constant mean = 3 Beverton-Holt = 4 Ricker Note: fit_wham allows fitting SCAA model (NAA_re = NULL), estimates recruitment every year separate fixed effect parameters, case stock-recruit function estimated. warning message printed recruit_model > 2 NAA_re = NULL. wish use stock-recruit function expected recruitment, choose recruitment deviations random effects, either age-1 (NAA_re = list(sigma="rec")) ages (NAA_re = list(sigma="rec+1"), "full state-space" model). See details NAA_re specification. ecov specifies environmental covariate data models. See set_ecov full details. selectivity specifies options selectivity, overwrite existing options specified ASAP data file. See set_selectivity full details. M specifies estimation options natural mortality can overwrite M--age values specified ASAP data file. See set_M full details. NAA_re specifies options random effects numbers--age (NAA, .e. state-space model ). See set_NAA full details. NULL, traditional statistical catch--age model fit. catchability specifies options catchability. See set_q full details. NULL asap3 NULL, single catchability parameter index used initial values specified ASAP file. NULL, initial catchabilities indices = 0.3. move specifies options movement multiple regions. See set_move full details. age_comp specifies age composition models fleet(s) indices. See set_age_comp full details. catch_info optional list fishery catch information can used set types observations asap3 file given.  See set_catch full details. Useful setting operating model simulate population processes observations. Also can useful setting structure assessment model asap3 used. index_info optional list survey/index information can used set types observations asap3 file given.  See set_indices full details. Useful setting operating model simulate population processes observations. Also can useful setting structure assessment model asap3 used. basic_info optional list information can used set population types observations asap3 file given. Particularly useful setting operating model simulate population processes observations. Also can useful setting structure assessment model asap3 used. current options : $ages integer vector ages (years) last plus group $years integer vector years population model spans. $n_seasons number seasons within year. $n_fleets number fishing fleets. $fracyr_seasons proportions year season within year (sums 1). $F matrix (length(years) x n_fleets) annual fishing mortality rates fleet initialize model. $waa array ((n_fleets + n_indices + n_stocks) x length(years) x length(ages)) annual weight age fleet, index, spawning biomass stock. $maturity array (n_stocks x length(years) x length(ages)) annual maturity age estimating spawning biomass stock. $fracyr_SSB matrix (n_years x n_stocks) (1 length(years)) yearly proportions (0-1) year calculate spawning biomass. $spawn_seasons vector (n_stocks) seasons stock spawns. $spawn_regions vector (n_stocks) regions stock spawns. $NAA_where array (n_stocks x n_regions x n_ages) 0/1 indicating individuals stock may exist January 1 year. $Fbar_ages integer vector ages use average F age reported "Fbar" across fleets, regions fleet. $q vector (length(n_indices)) catchabilities indices initialize model. $percentSPR (0-100) percentage unfished spawning biomass per recruit determining equilibrium fishing mortality reference point $XSPR_input_average_years years average inputs per recruit calculation (selectivity, M, WAA, maturity) static (prevailing) SPR-based reference points. Default last 5 years (tail(1:length(years),5)). specific values override option. $average_years_L list (length = n_regions), years average L (extra mortality) per recruit calculation static (prevailing) reference points. Default last 5 years (tail(1:length(years),5)) $average_years_M list (length = n_stocks, list length = n_regions), years average M per recruit calculation static (prevailing) reference points. Default last 5 years (tail(1:length(years),5)) $average_years_move list (length = n_stocks, list length = n_regions), years average movement parameters per recruit calculation static (prevailing) reference points. Default last 5 years (tail(1:length(years),5)) $average_years_mat list (length = n_stocks), years average maturity per recruit calculation static (prevailing) reference points. Default last 5 years (tail(1:length(years),5)) $average_years_waassb list (length = n_stocks), years average ssb waa per recruit calculation static (prevailing) reference points. Default last 5 years (tail(1:length(years),5)) $average_years_sel list (length = n_fleets), years average selectivity per recruit calculation static (prevailing) reference points. Default last 5 years (tail(1:length(years),5)) $average_years_waacatch list (length = n_fleets), years average catch waa per recruit calculation static (prevailing) reference points. Default last 5 years (tail(1:length(years),5)) $average_years_SRR list (length = n_stocks), years average SRR parameters static (prevailing) MSY-based reference points. Default last 5 years (tail(1:length(years),5)) $XSPR_R_avg_yrs years average recruitments calculating SPR-based SSB reference points. Default 1:length(years) $XSPR_R_opt 1(3): use annual R estimates(predictions) annual SSB_XSPR, 2(4): use average R estimates(predictions). 5: use bias-corrected expected recruitment. long-term projections, may important use certain years XSPR_R_avg_yrs $FXSPR_init F initialize internal newton search annual static F X percent SPR. Default 0.5 $FMSY_init F initialize internal newton search annual static Fmsy (stock-recruit model assumed). Default 0.5 $simulate_process_error T/F vector (length = 9). simulating model, whether simulate process errors     (NAA, M, selectivity, q, movement, unidentified mortality, q priors, movement priors, Ecov). used applicable random effects. $simulate_observation_error T/F vector (length = 3). simulating model, whether simulate  catch, index, ecov observations. $simulate_period T/F vector (length = 2). simulating model, whether simulate base period (model years) projection period. $bias_correct_process T/F. Perform bias correction log-normal random effects NAA. $bias_correct_BRPs T/F. Perform bias correction analytic SSB/R Y/R bias correction log-normal NAA. May want use XSPR_R_opt = 5 long-term projections. arguments prepare_wham_input provided selectivity, M, age_comp, information provided must consistent basic_info. example dimensions number years, ages, fleets, indices.

title: Prepare input data and parameters for WHAM model — prepare_wham_input
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ asap3 = read_asap3_dat("ex1_SNEMAYT.dat") input = prepare_wham_input(asap3) mod = fit_wham(input)  # no ASAP3 file, default parameter values and configuration input = prepare_wham_input() mod = fit_wham(input, fit = FALSE) set.seed(8675309) simdata = mod$simulate(complete=TRUE) input$data = simdata fit = fit_wham(input, do.osa = FALSE) } # }

Use change default call project_wham suggests . $proj_F_init F initialize internal newton search annual projected F given user-specifed catch. Default 0.1 $avg.yrs.sel list (length = n_fleets), years average selectivity FAA fleet projection years. BRPs calculated projection years also use . Default = last 5 years, tail(model$years, 5). $avg.yrs.waacatch list (length = n_fleets), years average weight age fleet projection years ($proj_waa NULL). BRPs calculated projection years also use . Default = last 5 years, tail(model$years, 5). $avg.yrs.waassb list (length = n_stocks), years average weight age stock SSB projection years ($proj_waa NULL). BRPs calculated projection years also use . Default = last 5 years, tail(model$years, 5). $avg.yrs.mature list (length = n_stocks), years average maturity age stock projection years ($proj_mature NULL). BRPs calculated projection years also use . Default = last 5 years, tail(model$years, 5). $avg.yrs.L list (length = n_regions), years average extra mortality age region projection years ($cond.L.re = FALSE). BRPs calculated projection years also use . Default = last 5 years, tail(model$years, 5). $avg.yrs.M list (length = n_stocks, list length = n_regions), years average natural mortality age stock region projection years ($cont.M.re = FALSE). BRPs calculated projection years also use . Default = last 5 years, tail(model$years, 5). $avg.yrs.move list (length = n_stocks, list length = n_regions), years average movement rates age season stock region (beginning interval) projection years ($cont.move.re = FALSE). BRPs calculated projection years also use . Default = last 5 years, tail(model$years, 5). $avg.yrs.R list (length = n_stocks), years average recruitment deviations stock region projection years ($proj_R_opt = 3). BRPs calculated projection years also use . Default = last 5 years, tail(model$years, 5). $avg.yrs.NAA list (length = n_stocks, list length = n_regions), years average NAA deviations stock region projection years ($proj_NAA_opt = 2). BRPs calculated projection years also use . Default = last 5 years, tail(model$years, 5). n.newton integer, number additional Newton steps optimization. Passed fit_tmb. Default = 0 projections. .sdrep T/F, calculate standard deviations model parameters? See sdreport. Default = TRUE. MakeADFun.silent T/F, Passed silent argument TMB::MakeADFun. Default = FALSE. save.sdrep T/F, save full TMB::sdreport object? FALSE, save summary.sdreport reduce model object file size. Default = TRUE. check.version T/F check whether version WHAM TMB fitted model match version WHAM using projections. Default = TRUE. TMB.bias.correct T/F whether use bias.correct feature TMB::sdreport. Default = FALSE. TMB.jointPrecision T/F whether return joint precision matrix fixed random effects TMB::sdreport. Default = FALSE.

title: Project a fit WHAM model — project_wham
text: projected WHAM model additional output specified: $rep List derived quantity estimates (see examples) $sdrep Parameter estimates (standard errors .sdrep=TRUE) $peels Retrospective analysis (.retro=TRUE) $osa One-step-ahead residuals (.osa=TRUE)

title: Project a fit WHAM model — project_wham
text: WHAM implements five options handling fishing mortality projections. Exactly one must specified proj.opts: Use last year F (default). Set proj.opts$use.last.F = TRUE. WHAM use F terminal model year projections. Use average F. Set proj.opts$use.avg.F = TRUE. WHAM use F averaged proj.opts$avg.yrs projections (done M-, maturity-, weight--age). Use F X% SPR. Set proj.opts$use.FXSPR = TRUE. WHAM calculate F X% SPR. Specify F. Provide proj.opts$proj.F, F vector length = n.yrs. Specify catch. Provide proj.opts$proj.catch, vector aggregate catch length = n.yrs. WHAM calculate F get specified catch. proj.opts$avg.yrs controls years following averaged projections: Maturity--age Weight--age Natural mortality--age Fishing mortality--age (proj.opts$use.avgF = TRUE) fitting model recruitment estimated freely year, .e. fixed effects ASAP, WHAM handles recruitment projection years similarly using empirical cumulative distribution function. WHAM calculating mean standard deviation log(R) model years (default) specified subset years (proj.opts$avg.rec.yrs). WHAM treats recruitment projections random effect mean SD, .e. log(R) ~ N(meanlogR, sdlogR). WHAM implements four options handling environmental covariate(s) projections. Exactly one must specified proj.opts ecov model: (Default) Continue ecov process model (e.g. random walk, AR1) Set $cont.ecov = TRUE. WHAM estimate ecov process projection years (.e. continue random walk / AR1 process). Use last year ecov(s) Set $use.last.ecov = TRUE. WHAM use ecov value terminal year (population model) projections. Use average ecov(s) Provide $avg.yrs.ecov, vector specifying years average environmental covariate(s) projections. Specify ecov Provide $proj.ecov, matrix user-specified environmental covariate(s) use projections. Dimensions must # projection years (proj.opts$n.yrs) x # ecovs (ncols(ecov$mean)). original model fit ecov years beyond population model, WHAM use already-fit ecov values projections. ecov model extended least proj.opts$n.yrs years beyond population model, none need specified.

title: Project a fit WHAM model — project_wham
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ data("input4_SNEMAYT") # load SNEMA yellowtail flounder input data and model settings mod <- fit_wham(input4_SNEMAYT) # using default values (do.proj=T)  mod2 <- fit_wham(input4_SNEMAYT, do.retro=F, do.osa=F, do.proj=F) # fit model without projections, retro analysis, or OSA residuals mod_proj <- project_wham(mod2) # add projections to previously fit model, using default values: use.lastF = TRUE, n.yrs = 3, avg.yrs = last 5 years  names(mod_proj$rep) # list of derived quantities tail(mod_proj$rep$SSB, 3) # get 3-year projected SSB estimates (weight, not numbers)  x <- summary(mod_proj$sdrep) unique(rownames(x)) # list of estimated parameters and derived quanitites with SE x <- x[rownames(x) == "log_SSB",] # SSB estimates with SE ssb.mat <- exp(cbind(x, x[,1] + qnorm(0.975)*cbind(-x[,2],x[,2])))/1000 # calculate 95% CI colnames(ssb.mat) <- c("SSB","SSB_se","SSB_lower","SSB_upper") tail(ssb.mat, 3) # 3-year projected SSB estimates with SE and 95% CI } # }

title: Read ASAP3 fit — read_asap3_fit
text: Gets output fit ASAP3 model plotting WHAM models.

title: Read ASAP3 fit — read_asap3_fit
code: read_asap3_fit(wd, asap.name, pSPR = 40)

title: Read ASAP3 fit — read_asap3_fit
text: wd character, directory ASAP3 output files located (ex: 'C://file/directories/model/'). 5 files needed: .rdat, .dat, .std, .cor, .par. asap.name character, base name original .dat file (.e. without .dat extension) pSPR scalar, user-specified percent SPR use reference points, expressed 100*SSBPR(Fspr)/SSBPR(F=0). Default = 40.

title: Read ASAP3 fit — read_asap3_fit
text: named list following elements: $years numeric vector, model years , e.g. 1972:2020 $years_full numeric vector, model + proj years, e.g. 1972:2022. ASAP $years. $selAA list length(n_selblocks), first fleet blocks indices, .e. 4 fleet blocks 3 indices, selAA[[5]] index 1. element matrix, years (rows) x ages (cols), selectivity age $selblock_pointer_fleets matrix, n_years x n_fleets, indices selAA used fleet year $selblock_pointer_indices matrix, n_years x n_indices, indices selAA used index year $MAA matrix, n_years x n_ages, natural mortality $log_SSB matrix, n_years x 2, log-scale spawning stock biomass. 1st col = MLE, 2nd col = SE (.std file ADMB). $log_F matrix, n_years x 2, log-scale fully-selected F. 1st col = MLE, 2nd col = SE (.std file ADMB). $log_NAA matrix, n_years x n_ages, numbers age $NAA_CV matrix, n_years x n_ages, CV numbers age $percentSPR scalar, X% SPR used calculate reference points, default = 40 $log_Y_FXSPR matrix, n_years x 2, log-scale yield FXSPR. 1st col = MLE, 2nd col = SE. $log_FXSPR matrix, n_years x 2, log-scale FXSPR. 1st col = MLE, 2nd col = SE. $log_SSB_FXSPR matrix, n_years x 2, log-scale SSB FXSPR, .e. annual numerator SPR(FXSPR) * Recruits. 1st col = MLE, 2nd col = SE. $log_rel_ssb_F_cov list, length n_years, element 2x2 covariance matrix SSB / SSB_FXSPR first F / F_FXSPR second

title: Read ASAP3 fit — read_asap3_fit
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ base <- read_asap3_fit(wd=file.path(getwd(),'asap_results'), asap.name='BASE_5C.DAT', pSPR=40) m1 <- fit_wham(input1) m2 <- fit_wham(input2) mods <- list(base=base, m1=m1, m2=m2) res <- compare_wham_models(mods) } # }

Useful self test stages. conditional T/F whether fix rather simulate estimated random effects. what: Specify the age composition models for fleet(s) and indices. — set_age_comp
title: Specify the age composition models for fleet(s) and indices. — set_age_comp
text: Specify age composition models fleet(s) indices.

title: Specify the age composition models for fleet(s) and indices. — set_age_comp
code: set_age_comp(input, age_comp)

title: Specify the age composition models for fleet(s) and indices. — set_age_comp
text: input list containing data, parameters, map, random elements (output wham::prepare_wham_input) age_comp specifies age composition models fleet(s) indices. NULL, multinomial used option ASAP. age composition models available : "multinomial" Multinomial. default option ASAP. 0 parameters. "dir-mult" Saturating Dirichlet-multinomial, parameterized effective-sample-size nonlinear saturating function respect input-sample-size. 1 parameter. Effective sample size estimated model (Candy 2008) "dirichlet-pool0" Dirichlet, pooling zero observations adjacent age classes. 1. parameter. See Francis 2014 Albertsen et al. 2016 "dirichlet-miss0"  "logistic-normal-miss0" Logistic normal, treating zero observations missing. 1 parameter. "logistic-normal-ar1-miss0" Logistic normal, treating zero observations missing. 1 parameter. "logistic-normal-pool0" Logistic normal, pooling zero observations adjacent age classes. 1 parameter. See Schnute Haigh (2007) Francis (2014) "logistic-normal-01-infl" Zero--one inflated logistic normal. Inspired zero-one inflated beta Ospina Ferrari (2012). 3 parameters. . OSA residuals. "logistic-normal-01-infl-2par" Zero-one inflated logistic normal p0 function binomial sample size. 2 parameters. OSA residuals. "mvtweedie" Multivariate-tweedie, product composition proportions input sample sizes follows distribution mean equal product predicted proportions input sample size, parameters define ratio effective input sample size (bounded 0 Inf) probability zeros. 2 parameters. OSA residuals. "dir-mult-linear" Linear Dirichlet-multinomial, parameterized effective-sample-size linear function respect input-sample-size, estimating 1 parameter, \(log(\theta)\), ratio effective input sample size approximately \(\theta / (1+\theta)\), .e., logistic transformation estimated parameter \(log(\theta)\).  (Thorson et al. 2017) two Dirichlet-multinomial options differ input-sample-size differs among years.  cases, linear-Dirichlet multinomial designed decrease effective sample size year approximately proportion, saturating-Dirichlet multinomial decrease years highest input-sample-size much lower input-sample-size. One-step-ahead residuals calculated options 8-10 .osa=TRUE (Nielsen et al. prep.). age composition model needs specified fleet index. like fleets indices use age composition likelihood, can simply specify one strings , .e. age_comp = "logistic-normal-miss0". want age composition model used fleets indices, must specify named list following entries: $fleets vector strings length = number fleets. $indices vector strings length = number indices.

title: Specify the age composition models for fleet(s) and indices. — set_age_comp
text: named list elements input provided age composition likelihood options modified.

title: Specify the age composition models for fleet(s) and indices. — set_age_comp
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ wham.dir <- find.package("wham") path_to_examples <- system.file("extdata", package="wham") asap3 <- read_asap3_dat(file.path(path_to_examples,"ex1_SNEMAYT.dat")) input <- prepare_wham_input(asap3) input <- set_age_comp(input, age_comp = "logistic-normal-miss0") #no longer multinomial } # }

title: Specify configuration for environmental covariates, effects on the population, and parameter values — set_ecov
code: set_ecov(input, ecov)

title: Specify configuration for environmental covariates, effects on the population, and parameter values — set_ecov
text: input list containing data, parameters, map, random elements (output wham::prepare_wham_input) ecov (optional) named list environmental covariate data parameters (see details) ecov specifies environmental covariate data model well effect population. Environmental covariate data need span years fisheries data. can NULL environmental data fit. Otherwise, must named list following components: $label Name(s) environmental covariate(s). Used printing. $mean Mean observations (matrix). number years x number covariates. Missing values = NA. $logsigma Configure observation standard errors. Options: Matrix \(log\) standard errors dimensions $mean Specified values time step log standard errors covariate, numeric vector matrix w/ dim 1 x n.ecov Specified value time steps estimation option (covariates). character string: "est_1": Estimated, one value shared among time steps.           "est_re": Estimated value time step random effects two parameters (mean, var) list two elements. First matrix log standard errors vector single values covariate .           Second character vector estimation options (NA, "est_1","est_re") covariate.           covariates non-NA values, values first element ignored.  $year Years corresponding observations (vector length $mean $logsigma) $use_obs T/F (1/0) vector/matrix dimension $mean $logsigma.     Use observation? Can used ignore subsets ecov without changing data files. $process_model Process model ecov time-series. "rw" = random walk, "ar1" = 1st order autoregressive,        NA = fit $process_mean_vals vector (initial) mean values ecov time-series. $process_sig_vals vector (initial) standard deviation values ecov time-series. $process_cor_vals vector (initial) correlation values ecov time-series. $recruitment_how character matrix (n_Ecov x n_stocks) indicating ecov affects recruitment stock.        Options based (see Iles & Beverton (1998))        combined order orthogonal polynomial covariate form "type-lag-order". "type" can : = "none" effect. = "controlling" pre-recruit density-independent mortality. = "limiting" maximum recruitment, e.g. ecov determines amount suitable habitat) = "lethal" threshold, .e. R –> 0 ecov value. = "masking" metabolic/growth, decreases dR/dS = "directive" e.g. behavioral type "none", "lag" can : = "lag-n" lag = n can 0,1,2,.... lag-1 implies covariate year y affects recruitment year y+1. "type" "none", "order" can : = "linear" covariate effect linear transformed recruitment parameter (e.g., log). = "poly-n" orthogonal polynomial n = 1 ("linear"),2,... "limiting-lag-1-poly-2" model covariate affecting recruitment next year (lag = 1) second order orthogonal          polynomial represent (\(b_0 + b_1*\mathrm{Ecov} + b_2*\mathrm{Ecov} + ...\)) limiting effect. $M_how character array (n_Ecov x n_stocks x n_ages x n_regions) indicating ecov affects M age,stock,region        form "lag-order". "lag" can : = "none" effect. = "lag-n" lag = n can 0,1,2,.... lag-1 implies covariate year y affects M year y+1. "lag" "none", "order" can : = "linear" covariate effect linear transformed M parameter (e.g., log). = "poly-n" orthogonal polynomial n = 1 ("linear"),2,...  $M_effect_map integer array (n_stocks x n_ages x n_regions x n_Ecov) indicating estimated effects common age,stock,region.       specified effect estimated M $M_how "none" covariate. $q_how character matrix (n_Ecov x n_indices) indicating whether ecov affects catchability index.      form "lag-order". "lag" can : = "none" effect. = "lag-n" lag = n can 0,1,2,.... lag-1 implies covariate year y affects catchability year y+1. "lag" "none", "order" can : = "linear" covariate effect linear transformed catchability parameter (e.g., log). = "poly-n" orthogonal polynomial n = 1 ("linear"),2,...  $move_how character array (n_Ecov x n_stocks x n_ages x n_seasons x n_regions x n_regions - 1) indicating whether ecov        affects movement one region others stock,age,season. form "lag-order". "lag" can : = "none" effect. = "lag-n" lag = n can 0,1,2,.... lag-1 implies covariate year y affects movement parameter year y+1. "lag" "none", "order" can : = "linear" covariate effect linear transformed movement parameter (e.g., log). = "poly-n" orthogonal polynomial n = 1 ("linear"),2,...  $move_effect_map integer array (n_stocks x n_ages x n_seasons x n_regions x n_regions-1 x n_Ecov) indicating estimated        effects common age,stock,region, season etc. specified effect estimated movement parameters        $move_how "none" covariate. $beta_R_vals n_stocks x n_ecov x max(n_poly_R) array initial values effects recruitment. $beta_M_vals n_stocks x n_ages x n_regions x n_ecov x max(n_poly_M) array initial values effects natural mortality. $beta_q_vals n_indices x n_ecov x max(n_poly_q) array initial values effects catchability. $beta_mu_vals n_stocks x n_ages x n_seasons x n_regions x n_regions - 1 x n_ecov x max(n_poly_move) array initial values effects movement parameters.

title: Specify configuration for environmental covariates, effects on the population, and parameter values — set_ecov
text: named list elements input provided environmental covariate observations, effects, model options modified.

title: Specify configuration for environmental covariates, effects on the population, and parameter values — set_ecov
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ wham.dir <- find.package("wham") path_to_examples <- system.file("extdata", package="wham") asap3 <- read_asap3_dat(file.path(path_to_examples,"ex1_SNEMAYT.dat")) env.dat <- read.csv(file.path(path_to_examples,"GSI.csv"), header=T) input <- prepare_wham_input(asap3, NAA_re = list(sigma = "rec")) ecov <- list(  label = "GSI",  mean = as.matrix(env.dat$GSI),  logsigma = 'est_1', # estimate obs sigma, 1 value shared across years  year = env.dat$year,  use_obs = matrix(1, ncol=1, nrow=dim(env.dat)[1]), # use all obs (=1)  process_model = 'ar1') # "rw" or "ar1" input <- set_ecov(input, ecov = ecov) #GSI in the model without any effects } # }

what: Specify configuration for fully-selected fishing mortality — set_F

text: input list containing data, parameters, map, random elements (output prepare_wham_input) index_info (optional) list specifying various aspects catch indices (see details) index_info specifies observations, various configuration options index-specific catch observations overwrite attributes specified ASAP data file. NULL, settings ASAP data file basic_info used. index_info list following entries: $n_indices number indices $index_regions vector (n_indices) regions fleet operates. $index_seasons vector (n_indices) 0/1 values flagging seasons index occurs. $index_names character vector (n_indices) names indices Used naming results plots tables. $agg_indices matrix (n_years_model x n_indices) annual aggregate index catches. $agg_index_cv matrix (n_years_model x n_indices) CVs annual aggregate index catches. $fracyr_indices matrix (n_years_model x n_indices) fractions year index occurs within season (difference time survey time start season). $use_indices matrix (n_years_model x n_indices) 0/1 values flagging whether use aggregate observations. $units_indices matrix (n_years_model x n_indices) 1/2 values flagging whether aggregate observations biomass (1) numbers (2). $index_paa array (n_indices x n_years_model x n_ages) annual catch proportions age index. $use_index_paa matrix (n_years_model x n_indices) 0/1 values flagging whether use proportions age observations. $units_index_paa matrix (n_years_model x n_indices) 1/2 values flagging whether composition observations biomass (1) numbers (2). $index_Neff matrix (n_years_model x n_indices) effective sample sizes proportions age observations. $waa_pointer_indices vector (n_indices) itegers indicated waa use index. $selblock_pointer_indices matrix (n_years_model x n_indices) itegers indicated selblocks use. $initial_index_sd_scale vector (n_indices) scalar multipliers annual log-observation standard deviation. Default = 1. $map_index_sd_scale integer vector (n_indices) specifying sd scalar parameters fix. Use NA fix parameter integers estimate.         Use integer multiple indices estimate shared scalar parameter.

title: Specify model and parameter configuration for natural mortality — set_M
text: input list containing data, parameters, map, random elements (output wham::prepare_wham_input) M (optional) list specifying natural mortality options: model, random effects, initial values, parameters fix (see details) M specifies estimation options natural mortality can overwrite M--age values specified ASAP data file. NULL, M--age matrix ASAP data file used (M fixed, estimated). estimate M--age shared/mirrored among ages, modify M$means_map (see vignette details). M list following entries: $mean_model Character describing type model M stock regional models natural mortality. Options : "fixed-M" Use initial values ASAP3 dat files $initial_means (mean) M fixed values. ASAP3 files           $initial_means provided, default M = 0.2 stocks, regions ages "estimate-M" estimate one (mean) M parameters. Default estimate single M shared across stocks ages,           use $means_map fix estimate parameters specific stocks, regions, ages. "weight--age" specifies M function weight--age, \(M_{y,} = \mathrm{exp}(b0 + b1*\mathrm{log}(W_{y,}))\),           Lorenzen (1996)           Miller & Hyun (2018).           Default estimate single model shared across stocks regions,           use $means_map[s,r,1] fix estimate intercept specific stocks, regions. See also $logb_prior           $initial_b configuring slope log scale.  $initial_means array (n_stocks x n_regions x n_ages) initial/mean M stock, region age. NULL, initial       mean M--age values given stock region taken first row MAA matrix ASAP data file.        ASAP data file, M = 0.2 default. $mean_model "weight--age"       elements first age ($initial_means[,,1]) used (intercept log(M)). $means_map array (n_stocks x n_regions x n_ages) NA integers ( 0 <= max <= n_stocks * n_regions * n_ages) indicating       ages estimate (mean) M whether set ages identical. E.g. model 2 stock, 2 regions       6 ages constant M estimated stock across regions ages  $M_ages_map[1,,] = 1       $M_ages_map[2,,] = 2. $M_ages_map[1,1,] = c(NA,1,1,2,2,3) fix M age 1 initial value,       estimates ages 2 3 identical ages 4 5 different age 6+ stock 1       region 1. NULL, specifies ages fixed M$initial_means.  $mean_model "weight--age"       used stocks regions elements first age ($M_ages_map[,,1])       used (intercept log(M)). $intial_MAA array (n_stocks x n_regions x n_years x n_ages) initial values M age. Intended uses nothing pertaining M estimated. $b_model "constant","stock","region", "stock-region" defining whether parameter constant, stock-specific, region-specific,       stock- region-specific. used M$mean_model = "weight--age". $b_prior T/F, N(mu, 0.08) prior (mu = log(0.305) default) used log_b? Based Fig. 1 Table 1       (marine fish)  Lorenzen (1996). (used       $mean_model "weight--age"). $intial_b elements $mean_model "weight--age", initial value mean b weight--age       model. $re_model Character matrix (n_stocks x n_regions) options time- age-varying (random effects) M stock region.       Possible values : "none" (default) random effects age year. "iid_a" uncorrelated M age, constant time. "iid_y" uncorrelated M year, constant ages. "ar1_a" M correlated age (AR1), constant time. "ar1_y" M correlated year (AR1), constant ages. "iid_ay" M uncorrelated year age (2D). "ar1_ay" M correlated year age (2D AR1), Cadigan           (2016).  $re_map array (n_stocks x n_regions x n_ages) NA integers (1 <= max <= n_ages) indicating ages, given       stock region, random effects (NA) whether set RE ages identical. E.g. model 2 stock,       2 regions 6 ages, $re_map[2,1,] = c(NA,1,1,2,2,3) estimate RE age 1, ages       2 3 identical ages 4 5 different age 6+ stock 2 region 1. NULL,       $re_model specifies M random effects age, least two ages must       specified correlation among ages estimated. $sigma_vals n_stocks x n_regions matrix Initial standard deviation value use M random effects. Values used       M$re_model = "none". Otherwise, single value. unspecified values 0.1. $cor_vals n_stocks x n_regions x 2 array initial correlation values use M deviations. unspecified initial       values 0. M$re_model = "iid_a", "iid_y", "iid_ay" "none" values used. "ar1_a" first value cor_vals[s,r,1] used. "ar1_y" second value cor_vals[s,r,2] used. "ar1_ay" First "age", second "year".  $sigma_map n_stocks x n_region matrix NA integers indicating random effects sd estimated whether       set identical. supplied single sd estimated stock region $re_model "none". $cor_map n_stocks x n_region matrix x 2 array NA integers indicating random effects correlation parameters estimated       whether set identical. supplied single value age /year estimated stock region       $re_model "none", "iid_a", "iid_y".

title: Specify model and parameter configuration for natural mortality — set_M
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ wham.dir <- find.package("wham") path_to_examples <- system.file("extdata", package="wham") asap3 <- read_asap3_dat(file.path(path_to_examples,"ex1_SNEMAYT.dat")) input <- prepare_wham_input(asap3) M = list(mean_model = "estimate-M") input <- set_M(input, M = M) #estimate a constant M parameters } # }

title: Specify model and parameter configuration for movement when input$data$n_regions > 1 — set_move
text: input list containing data, parameters, map, random elements (output wham::prepare_wham_input) move (optional) list specifying movement options: model, random effects, initial values, parameters fix (see details) move specifies estimation options movement. NULL, movement occur. multiple regions, stock modeled separately different regions without movement. move list following entries: $stock_move length = n_stocks, T/F whether stock can move. provided movement defined stocks. $separable length = n_stocks, T/F whether movement modeled separably mortality occuring simultaneously. $mean_model matrix (n_regions x (n_regions-1)): model options fixed effects (mean possibly variance) movement parameter : "none" (default) movement regions. "constant" estimate single movement rate region shared across stocks, seasons, ages, years "season" estimate movement rates  region season shared across stocks, ages, years "stock_constant" estimate movement rate stock region shared across seasons, ages, years "stock_season" estimate movement rate stock season region shared across ages, years  $age_re matrix (n_regions x (n_regions-1)): options age random effects (mean parameter defined move$mean_model): "none" (default) movement rate random effects age. "iid" independent movement rate random effects age. "ar1" allow first order autoregressive correlation movement rate random effects age.  $year_re matrix (n_regions x (n_regions-1)): options yearly random effects (mean parameter defined move$mean_model): "none" (default) movement rate random effects year. "iid" independent movement rate random effects year. "ar1" allow first order autoregressive correlation movement rate random effects year.  $prior_sigma array (n_stocks x n_seasons x n_regions x n_regions - 1) sd parameters normal priors mean movement parameters transformed scale (-Inf,Inf) $use_prior array (n_stocks x n_seasons x n_regions x n_regions - 1) 0/1 indicator whether include prior mean movement parameters joint log-likelihood. $can_move array (n_stocks x n_seasons x n_regions x n_regions) 0/1 indicator whether movement can occur one region another. $must_move array (n_stocks x n_seasons x n_regions) 0/1 indicator whether movement region must occur. $mean_vals array (n_stocks x n_seasons x n_regions x n_regions-1) initial movement rate parameters ** region. Usage depends move$mean_model. $sigma_vals array (n_stocks x n_seasons x n_regions x n_regions -1) initial standard deviations use random effects. Usage depends move$age_re move$year_re. $cor_vals array (n_stocks x n_seasons x n_regions x n_regions - 1x 2) initial correlation values use random effects. Usage depends move$age_re move$year_re.        cor_vals[,,,,1] correlation age, cor_vals[,,,,2] correlation year.

title: Specify model and parameter configuration for numbers at age — set_NAA
text: input list containing data, parameters, map, random elements (output prepare_wham_input) NAA_re (optional) list specifying options numbers--age random effects, initial parameter values, recruitment model (see details) NAA_re = NULL, traditional statistical catch--age model fit (NAA = pred_NAA ages, deterministic). Otherwise, NAA_re specifies numbers--age configuration. list following possible entries: $decouple_recruitment T/F determining whether correlation structure recruitment independent RE deviations older ages        (default = TRUE). applicable NAA_re$sigma = "rec+1" correlation across ages specified. TRUE NAA_re$cor = "ar1_a", deviations ages>1        correlation structure. TRUE NAA_re$cor "ar1_y" "2dar1" separate year correlation parameters estimated recruitment older        ages. $sigma ages allow deviations predicted NAA given NAA previous time step? Must single character string described vector                    length n_stocks. length = 1, assumptions applied stocks. Options specified strings: "rec" Random effects recruitment (deviations), ages deterministic "rec+1" "Full state space" model 2 estimated sigma_a, one recruitment one shared among ages  $sigma_vals Array (n_stocks x n_regions x n_ages) initial standard deviation values use NAA deviations. Values used recruit_model = 1 NAA_re$sigma                  specified. age 1 spawning region used NAA_re$sigma = "rec".                  NAA_re$sigma_map defined, user must ensure configuration compatible NAA_re$sigma_vals $sigma_map can specify complex parameter configuration entering integer array (nstocks x n_regions x n_ages), entry points                   NAA_sigma use stock age. E.g., 2 stocks, 2 regions, 6 ages, array(rep(c(1,2,2,3,3,3), = 4),c(2,2,6)) estimate 3                   sigmas, recruitment (age-1) deviations sigma_R, ages 2-3 sharing sigma_2, ages 4-6 sharing sigma_3.                   parameters stocks across regions. user must sure compatible NAA_re$sigma configuration defined.                   Values used recruit_model = 1 NAA_re$sigma specified. $cor Correlation structure NAA deviations. Must single character string described vector                  length n_stocks. length = 1, assumptions applied stocks. Options : "iid" NAA deviations vary year age, uncorrelated. "ar1_a" NAA deviations correlated age (AR1). "ar1_y" NAA deviations correlated year (AR1). "2dar1" NAA deviations correlated year age (2D AR1).  $cor_vals Array (n_stocks x n_regions x 3) initial correlation values use NAA deviations. first value correspond correlation across age.                  second yearly correlation NAA deviations ages recruitment decoupled, otherwise just ages recruitemnt. third correlation annual                  recruitment deviations used recruitment decoupled. unspecified initial values 0. NAA_re$cor = "iid", values used.                  NAA_re$cor = "ar1_a", yearly correlation used, vice versa "ar1_y". $cor_map n_stocks x n_region matrix x 3 array NA integers indicating random effects correlation parameters estimated       whether set estimated identically. 3 values given stock region age, year ("survival"), year ("recruitment").       age years values used NAA_re$cor = "2dar1", appropriate values used NAA_re$cor = "ar1_a", "ar1_y".       NAA_re$decouple_recruitment = TRUE, third value used NAA_re$sigma = "rec" "rec+1".       NAA_re$decouple_recruitment = FALSE, second value used applies recruitment "survival" NAA_re$sigma = "rec+1",       just recruitment NAA_re$sigma = "rec". supplied stock-specific values age /year estimated regions       NAA_re$cor "none", "iid". $N1_model Character vector (n_stocks) determining way model initial numbers age: "age-specific-fe" (default) age- region-specific fixed effects parameters "equilibrium" 2 fixed effects parameters: initial recruitment instantaneous fishing mortality rate generate equilibruim abundance age. "iid-re" (default) age- region-specific iid random effects parameters. 2 parameters: mean sd log NAA "ar1-re" (default) age- region-specific random effects parameters. 3 parameters: mean sd, cor log NAA  $N1_pars (n_stocks x n_regions x n_ages) array. N1_model = 0, filled initial values use abundance age stock region first year.        N1_model = 1 (equilibrium assumption), first two values ages dimension used: (s,r,1) value recruitment stock (region) (s,r,2) fully-selected        equilibrium fishing mortality rate generating rest numbers age first year. $recruit_model Integer vector (n_stocks) determining model recruitment. Overrides recruit_model argument prepare_wham_input. Must make sure NAA_re$sigma, NAA_re$cor        ecov properly specified. 1 SCAA, estimating annual recruitments fixed effects. 2 estimating mean recruitment annual recruitments random effects 3 Beverton-Holt stock-recruitment annual recruitments random effects 4 Ricker stock-recruitment annual recruitments random effects  $recruit_pars list (length = n_stocks) vectors initial parameters recruitment model. $recruit_model 3 4, parameters "alpha" "beta".

title: Specify model and parameter configuration for catchability — set_q
text: input list containing data, parameters, map, random elements (output prepare_wham_input) catchability (optional) list specifying options numbers--age random effects, initial parameter values, recruitment model (see details) catchability specifies options catchability. NULL asap3 NULL, single catchability parameter index used initial values specified ASAP file. NULL, initial catchabilities indices = 0.3. Otherwise, list following optional entries: $re Time-varying (random effects) index. Vector length = number indices.                  entry catchability$re must one following options: "none" (default) constant "iid" vary year age/par, uncorrelated "ar1" correlated year (AR1)  $initial_q Initial catchabilities index. vector length = number indices. override values provided basic_info$q.        basic_info$q asap3 provided, default q values 0.3. $q_lower Lower bound catchabilities index. vector length = number indices. indices NULL components default lower values 0. $q_upper Upper bound catchabilities index. vector length = number indices. indices NULL components default lower values 1000. $prior_sd vector NA standard deviations use gaussian prior logit transform catchability parameter. Length = number indices.       Indices non-NA values mean logit q random effect mean determined logit transform catchability$initial_q       sigma standard error. $sigma_val Vector initial standard deviation values use annual random effects index. Values used q$re = "none". Otherwise, single value indices. $sigma_map Specify sigma parameters fix random effect deviations. Must vector length = number indices.                Use NA fix parameter integers estimate. Use integer multiple indices share sigma parameter.                used re = 'none' indices. $cor_vals Vector initial correlation values use annual random effects index. unspecified initial values 0. used q$re = "ar1" $cor_map Specify ar1 correlation parameters fix random effect deviations. Must vector length = number indices.                re = 'ar1', element (index) must single value. Use NA fix parameter integers estimate.                Use integer multiple indices share correlation parameter. used re = 'none' re = 'iid' indices.

code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ wham.dir <- find.package("wham") path_to_examples <- system.file("extdata", package="wham") asap3 <- read_asap3_dat(file.path(path_to_examples,"ex1_SNEMAYT.dat")) input <- prepare_wham_input(asap3) catchability <- list(re = c("iid", "none")) input <- set_q(input, catchability = catchability) #independent time-varying random effects on q for first survey. } # }

code: set_selectivity(input, selectivity)

title: Specify model and parameter configuration for selectivity — set_selectivity
text: input list containing data, parameters, map, random elements (output prepare_wham_input) selectivity (optional) list specifying options selectivity blocks, models, initial parameter values, parameter fixing/mapping, random effects (see details) set_selectivity specifies options selectivity allows overwrite existing options input list specified ASAP data file. selectivity = NULL, selectivity options input used. prepare_wham_input(..., selectivity=selectivity) calls set_selectivity(..., selectivity=selectivity). already created input prepare_wham_input, can also use set_selectivity(input, selectivity=selectivity) modify selectivity specification. selectivity list following entries: $model Selectivity model block. Vector length = number selectivity blocks. entry must one : "age-specific", "logistic", "double-logistic", "decreasing-logistic". $re Time-varying (random effects) block. Vector length = number selectivity blocks.                  NULL, selectivity parameters blocks constant time uncorrelated.                  entry selectivity$re must one following options, selectivity parameters : "none" (default) constant uncorrelated "iid" vary year age/par, uncorrelated "ar1" correlated age/par (AR1), year "ar1_y" correlated year (AR1), age/par "2dar1" correlated year age/par (2D AR1)  $initial_pars Initial parameter values block. List length = number selectivity blocks. entry must                vector length # parameters block, .e. c(2,0.2) logistic (a50 1/slope) c(0.5,0.5,0.5,1,1,0.5)                age-specific parameters 6 ages. Default set middle parameter range. 0.5 age-specific n.ages/2                logistic, double-logistic, decreasing-logistic. $fix_pars Alternative $map_pars specifying selectivity parameters (fixed effects) fix initial values.                List length = number selectivity blocks. E.g. model 3 age-specific blocks 6 ages,                list(4:5, 4, 2:4)) fix ages 4 5 block 1, age 4 block 2, ages 2, 3, 4 block 3.                Use NULL fix parameters block, e.g. list(NULL, 4, 2) fix pars block 1. $par_min lower bound selectivity parameters used populate data$selpars_lower.                List length = number selectivity blocks, item                vector length = number selectivity parameters (age-specific: n.ages, logistic: 2,                double-logistic: 4). $par_max upper bound selectivity parameters used populate data$selpars_upper.                List length = number selectivity blocks, item                vector length = number selectivity parameters (age-specific: n.ages, logistic: 2,                double-logistic: 4). $map_pars Alternative $fix_pars specifying fix selectivity parameters (fixed effects), corresponds                map$logit_selpars. List length = number selectivity blocks, item                vector length = number selectivity parameters (age-specific: n.ages, logistic: 2,                double-logistic: 4). Use NA fix parameter integers estimate. Use integer                multiple ages fleets/indices estimate shared parameter. E.g. model 3 age-specific                blocks (1 fleet, 2 indices) 6 ages, $map_pars = list(c(1,2,3,NA,NA,4), c(5,6,7,NA,8,8), c(1,2,3,NA,NA,4))                estimate ages 1-3 6 block 1 (fleet), ages 1-3 4-5 (shared) block 2 (index 1), set                index 2 (block 3) selectivity equal fleet. $sigma_vals Initial standard deviation values use random effect deviations. Must vector length = number blocks.                Use natural (log) scale, must positive. par$sel_repars[,1] estimated log-scale. used re = 'none' blocks. $map_sigma Specify SD parameters fix random effect deviations. Must vector length = number blocks.                Use NA fix parameter integers estimate. Use integer multiple blocks estimate shared SD parameter.                used re = 'none' blocks. $cor_vals Initial correlation values use random effect deviations. Must n_selblocks x 2 integer matrix. Columns correspond correlation age year,                respectively. re = 'ar1' re = 'ar1_y' corresponding values used.                Values must -1 1, parameters estimated logit transformed scale internally.                used re = 'none' re = 'iid' blocks. $map_cor Specify correlation parameters fix random effect deviations. Must n_selblocks x 2 matrix. Columns correspond correlation age                year,respectively. Parameters can shared setting corresponding values map_cor integer. Use NA fix parameter.                re = 'ar1' re = 'ar1_y', column corresponding correlation used.                used re = 'none' re = 'iid' blocks. $n_selblocks many selectivity blocks. Optional. unspecified asap3 object, set number                fleets + indices. specified, ensure components selectivity consistent.

title: Specify model and parameter configuration for selectivity — set_selectivity
text: named list elements input provided selectivity options modified.

title: Specify model and parameter configuration for selectivity — set_selectivity
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ wham.dir <- find.package("wham") path_to_examples <- system.file("extdata", package="wham") asap3 <- read_asap3_dat(file.path(path_to_examples,"ex1_SNEMAYT.dat")) input <- prepare_wham_input(asap3, NAA_re = list(sigma = "rec")) sel <- list(model=rep("logistic",input$data$n_selblocks),    initial_pars=rep(list(c(3,3)),input$data$n_selblocks),    fix_pars=rep(list(NULL),input$data$n_selblocks)) input <- set_selectivity(input, selectivity = sel) #logistic selectivity for all selectivity blocks } # }

title: wham 2.0.0 (2025-02-04)
text: Many new features developed previously lab devel branch attributed specific commit, now included main version WHAM Large changes structure input data, parameters, reported output may require editing workflows previous versions. 1 stocks regions can now modeled movement among regions. User can define seasonal intervals within years (possibly varying length). Seasonal operation fleets. Movement parameters stock, season, region--region specific. Movement parameters can incorporate time age varying random effects effects environmental covariates much like natural mortality. Priors mean movement parameters allowed currently possible include tagging data observations. Exported functions components model (e.g., set_NAA, set_catch, set_M, set_move) can used build model input sequentially modify previously created input. Greatly increased options functions allow user specify initial parameter values mapping parameter estimation. Random effects options initial abundance age. Functions perform jittering (jitter_wham) self tests (self_test). Functions add reference point estimation (do_reference_points), TMB::sdreport objects (do_sdrep), retrospective peels (do_retro_peels), OSA residuals (make_osa_residuals) previously fitted model. New Rmarkdown based generation html plot_wham_output self-contained (can opened location).
